races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Crownbreaker
runners.spotlight: 550,000gns yearling purchase whose siblings include July Cup winner Mill Stream; showed promise with close third of eight at Newmarket (5f, good to firm) on 1,000 Guineas day; sets a fairly useful standard.
runners.comment: Showed promise with close third of eight at Newmarket; sets a fairly useful standard

runners.name: Wild Blossom
runners.spotlight: 300,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; by Mehmas; dam maiden (RPR 63) half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Bayside Boy; stable landed this race with a newcomer (the subsequent Morny winner) last year; interesting contender.
runners.comment: 300,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; stable landed this race with a newcomer last year; interesting

runners.name: Miss Havisham
runners.spotlight: 400,000gns yearling; Starspangledbanner half-sister to three winners, notably Tamreer (Listed-placed 1m2f/1m4f; RPR 97); dam Group-placed 6f 2yo scorer (102); heed the market signals.
runners.comment: 400,000gns yearling; by Starspangledbanner out of 6f 2yo scorer; heed market signals
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Calef
runners.spotlight: 180,000gns yearling; Blue Point half-sister to two winners, notably Tiberius Thunder (Group-placed 1m AW; RPR 110); dam 5f-7f 2yo scorer (including Group 3; 110); the least expensive purchase in the field.
runners.comment: 180,000gns yearling; by Blue Point; the least expensive purchase in the field

races.race_name: Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Blue Lakota
runners.spotlight: Has something to prove back on turf but is a leading player provided AW form is transferred back to this sphere; runner-up off 10lb higher at Southwell (5f) most recently.
runners.comment: Leading player provided AW form is transferred back to turf; 10lb lower in this sphere

runners.name: Speeding Bullet
runners.spotlight: Had a solid spell (1422) in sprint contests last June-September; form dipped sharply on reappearance but he may rebound with the outing under his belt.
runners.comment: Form dipped on reappearance but he may rebound with the outing under his belt
runners.owner: Slaters Arms Racing And Ged Shields

runners.name: Fierce
runners.spotlight: Placed in AW contest on reappearance and has a fighting chance provided he builds on that effort; still well treated on his two wins (handicap/claimer) last summer.
runners.comment: Placed in AW contest on reappearance; well treated on his two wins last summer

runners.name: Harb
runners.spotlight: Hindered by traffic issues on his return to turf from AW but latest effort is harder to forgive; otherwise remains well treated on best form, albeit now goes back up in grade.
runners.comment: Latest effort is harder to forgive; well treated on best form but is back up in grade

runners.name: Dc Cogent
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner; 0-13 in handicaps but is in a consistent vein of form, most recently third at Nottingham (5f, good) returned to turf; possibilities off same mark.
runners.comment: 0-13 in handicaps but is in a consistent vein of form, third at Nottingham most recently
runners.owner: J R Dwyer And B Smart

runners.name: Harry Mole
runners.spotlight: Down the field at Haydock (6f) on handicap/seasonal debut, failing to back up his autumn novice form; however, may do better with the outing under his belt.
runners.comment: Down the field at Haydock on handicap/seasonal debut

runners.name: Wen Moon
runners.spotlight: Best to forgive reappearance effort (badly drawn) but he's not the percentage call unless the ground is soft; has gained all handicap wins on that surface.
runners.comment: Not the percentage call unless the ground is soft (all handicap wins on that surface)
runners.owner: Mps Racing, D Shapiro & D Clifford

runners.name: Imperial Guard
runners.spotlight: 0-10 for current stable; made the frame three times last May but wasn't in the same form afterwards in 2025; turnaround would give him a good chance off current mark.
runners.comment: 0-10 for current stable but a turnaround would give him a chance off current mark

runners.name: Acrisius
runners.spotlight: Opened his handicap account in Chester event (5f, good) last July and perhaps unsuited by softer going in two starts since; chance may depend on the weather's actions.
runners.comment: Won on good ground last July; perhaps unsuited by softer going in two runs since

runners.name: Keldeo
runners.spotlight: Has gained all handicap wins at Catterick/Pontefract and those two tracks have been the scene of her last two runs (finished first then fourth); makes first visit to Carlisle.
runners.comment: Has gained all handicap wins at Catterick/Pontefract; makes first visit to Carlisle
runners.owner: Ownaracehorse, O Pears and T&T Racing Ltd

runners.name: Hurt You Never
runners.spotlight: Best on good/firmer; 14-time winner who showed clear signs of a return to form at Ripon last time, finishing third; enters calculations off 6lb below last winning mark.
runners.comment: 14-time winner; showed clear signs of a revival last time; 6lb below last winning mark

races.race_name: Watch Racing TV Live Handicap

runners.name: Raft Up
runners.spotlight: In fine form at 6f on the AW of late, winning at Southwell and Newcastle; has a better strike-rate on sand than turf (1-17) but he was running to a similar level on grass as AW in 2025 and it may be unwise to presume he's a one-surface wonder; respected.
runners.comment: Won last 2 starts at 6f on AW; effective on turf so may translate that form back to grass

runners.name: Mr Dreamseller
runners.spotlight: Improved form when wide-margin winner of 6f novice on Wolverhampton AW in February; unable to replicate that form in Thirsk handicaps (6f, good) since, fitting a hood seemingly no help latest; needs much more.
runners.comment: Improved to win 6f novice on AW in February; well held in two turf handicaps since

runners.name: Mount Ruapehu
runners.spotlight: Three 6f wins on AW this year and he only narrowly failed to improve on a very modest turf strike-rate (now 1-35) when just touched off at Hamilton (5f, good) this month; no serious impact at Catterick (7f) since but ran well in headgear in Ireland and cheekpieces are now refitted.
runners.comment: Three 6f wins on AW this year; narrow 5f turf defeat this month; shade flat latest

runners.name: Counting Cards
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden; creditable show over 1m on Southwell AW when returning from absence last October but he dropped right out over 7f at Musselburgh (hood added, now discarded) recently; has question marks now but he had 6f form as a 2yo so the return to shorter is surely worth a go.
runners.comment: Maiden; has run well at 6f and 1m; weak finish at 7f latest; return to shorter worth a go

runners.name: Surprised
runners.spotlight: High in weights when first handicapping, following a Beverley novice win (about 7f) as 2yo, but was down 10lb from her opening mark when a never-nearer sixth at Catterick (6f, good) last week on second run for yard; this stiff test looks sure to suit and she has a good run in her.
runners.comment: Won Beverley novice (7f) as 2yo; promising Catterick run latest; this stiff test can suit

runners.name: Fan Mail
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins on AW and he's continued in reasonable form since the latest of them in October; has a 5lb lower turf mark but he didn't come up with much on grass last summer and this is the first time he's worn headgear.
runners.comment: Two 6f AW wins; in fair form on sand and has a 5lb lower turf mark but has a surface query
runners.owner: L Bond/Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi/Ptr

runners.name: Call Me Betty
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins at 7f and she ran respectably over that trip in turf handicaps late last summer but was too far below her best in November and on her reappearance last month to make much appeal today.
runners.comment: Two AW wins at 7f and respectable efforts in turf handicaps; way below best last 2 stsrts

runners.name: Lightning Galaxy
runners.spotlight: Not yet off the mark but he did run well in two 6f turf maidens (soft and good to firm) earlier in his career and the return to this trip may see more from him; this is competitive, though.
runners.comment: Has run well in 6f maidens earlier in career and the return to this trip might be a help

runners.name: Ran Amok
runners.spotlight: Both wins over 6f (good to soft and good), latterly at Doncaster last July when fending off one who won his next two, the pair clear; winless since but he had some excuses and is weighted to go well at this realistic level.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins; had some excuses since the latest one last July; weighted to go well

runners.name: Invincible Ruby
runners.spotlight: Broke duck over 7f at Catterick (good to firm) last July but nothing comparable since and has finished last in her two runs this year.
runners.comment: Broke duck over 7f at Catterick last July but nothing comparable since, including in 2026

runners.name: Magic Boy
runners.spotlight: Has led but was ridden more patiently when doing well to get up close home at Redcar (7f, good) in November, considering he was slightly checked in his run; up just 2lb in only his third handicap and has more to come with this stiff test at the trip unlikely to be a problem.
runners.comment: Arrived late to win at Redcar (7f) in November; up 2lb; has more to come this year

runners.name: Iris Dancer
runners.spotlight: Eleven turf wins, mostly at Hamilton but once over C&D, on ground ranging from soft to good to firm; weighted to find another race and will be suited by returning to a track with a stiff finish following her persevering fourth at Ayr 13 days ago; thereabouts.
runners.comment: Eleven turf wins include one C&D; weighted to find another race; staying-on 4th latest

runners.name: Impressor
runners.spotlight: Multiple sprint handicap wins since 2022, all on soft or slower; back in action with a good fourth of 17 at Doncaster in March and, although he hasn't done as well since, he'd come into it if there's significant rain.
runners.comment: All sprint handicap wins on soft or slower; back on last winning mark; wants rain

races.race_name: Follow Racing TV On X Handicap

runners.name: Loki
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo whose German middle-distance form featured one success and three second-place finishes; bred to stay further; sold for 42,000euros and gelded since last run; market instructive on British debut.
runners.comment: Low-mileage, ex-German 4yo who is making British debut; market instructive

runners.name: Ashdown Forest
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but could go well in refitted cheekpieces, having finished a close fifth in sole attempt in this headgear (penultimate start); didn't fare so well back in blinkers latest.
runners.comment: Still a maiden but could go well in refitted cheekpieces

runners.name: Charlie Darling
runners.spotlight: Has gradually found his feet on AW for new yard since returning from lengthy absence and gained neck wins at Southwell (2m) the last twice; at the top of his game and he was effective on turf in Ireland.
runners.comment: Gained neck wins on Southwell AW the last twice; at the top of his form

runners.name: As Fast As Wind
runners.spotlight: Consistent over hurdles for current yard; successful in last two Flat turf attempts, latest at Musselburgh (12.5f, good) last month returned to this code; 1-1 over 1m6f and warrants respect.
runners.comment: Successful in last two Flat turf attempts; 1-1 over 1m6f and commands respect

runners.name: Swinging London
runners.spotlight: Five wins on AW, including at Southwell (1m6f) last time; may remain competitive but looks by no means guaranteed to follow up, being 0-15 in Flat turf events; just 1-20 over hurdles.
runners.comment: By no means guaranteed to follow up latest AW win, being 0-15 in Flat turf races

runners.name: Lord
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in Catterick maiden (1m4f) last month in first Flat start in Britain; switches to handicap level off an attractive mark judged on his Listed hurdles form, which includes a success; possibilities.
runners.comment: Switches to handicap level off an attractive mark judged on his Listed hurdles form

runners.name: Billy No Mates
runners.spotlight: Rallied for third of six at Redcar (1m6f, good) on seasonal debut and remains well handicapped on historical data; just no certainty to step forward, being a veteran who hasn't won since 2023.
runners.comment: Veteran who isn't certain to take a step forward from reappearance effort

runners.name: Cascade Hall
runners.spotlight: Soon in a clear lead and perhaps stole a four-runner race at Ayr (1m7f, good to firm) on seasonal debut, belatedly opening his account; however, the 10l runner-up has franked the form by scoring since; double looks possible, provided everything again goes his way.
runners.comment: Perhaps stole the race at Ayr on reappearance but the runner-up has franked the form

runners.name: Haarar
runners.spotlight: Generally productive over hurdles but last few efforts leave a doubt over whether he'll be in top form returned to the Flat for first time since winning at Redcar (1m6f, soft) in November 2024.
runners.comment: Last few hurdles efforts leave a doubt over whether he'll be in top form

races.race_name: racingtv.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lunar Melody
runners.spotlight: 500,000euros breeze-up purchase a year ago; finished fifth at Carlisle (6f) in sole 2yo start then took a step forward with close second at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) on reappearance; has the best chance at the weights, with her sex allowance.
runners.comment: Fifth at Carlisle last term then close second at Doncaster on reappearance

runners.name: Wezzeer
runners.spotlight: Both starts over 6f on AW, justifying favouritism with half-length win at Southwell latest; not the figures pick under a penalty but is open to further progress and enters calculations; likely to stay 7f.
runners.comment: Won the latest of his two 6f AW runs and looks open to further progress

runners.name: Blue Boy Junior
runners.spotlight: Attracted support and duly showed promise at Thirsk (7f, good) where he finished fourth of 14, faring best of the newcomers; open to improvement and looks one of the main contenders.
runners.comment: Attracted support and duly showed promise at Thirsk, finishing fourth of 14

runners.name: Albemagic
runners.spotlight: Related to plenty of winners for his owner, notably 7f performer Myal, but they have tended to get better with experience, predominantly when handicapping; best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Family has tended to get better with experience; best watched on debut

runners.name: Bee Farmer
runners.spotlight: 68,000gns yearling, 90,000euros (breeze-up) 2yo; Mohaather half-brother to four winners, notably Motafaawit (Group-placed 5f-7.4f; RPR 115); dam 6f 2yo scorer (82), out of 1,000 Guineas winner Natagora; one of two newcomers in the field; check the betting.
runners.comment: Mohaather half-brother to four winners; one of two newcomers in the field

runners.name: Dark Sovereign
runners.spotlight: Both starts over 7f last August, finishing fourth to useful sort at Doncaster (good to firm) then failing to improve on soft ground at Sandown; gelded since; interesting on the debut form and may still do better.
runners.comment: Gelded since two 2yo runs; interesting on debut form and may still do better

runners.name: Golden Harvest
runners.spotlight: Not helped by a bad stumble at Leicester (7f; eased and finished last of 12) but was friendless in the betting and is probably one for handicaps later on.
runners.comment: Probably one for handicaps later on

runners.name: Freddie Steady Go
runners.spotlight: Bumper who has finished only seventh over 1m in both Flat starts; likely to find better opportunities in handicaps shortly.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who has finished only seventh in both Flat starts
runners.owner: Castle View, A Bell, S Oliver & M Severs

runners.name: Tzarmaggedon
runners.spotlight: Always behind in 7f AW contest at Newcastle; needs to step up markedly on that bare form.
runners.comment: Always behind in 7f AW contest at Newcastle
runners.owner: J R Dwyer And B Smart

runners.name: Royal Sizzler
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 16l over further on both 2yo starts; poor claims.
runners.comment: Poor claims on form

runners.name: Great Profit
runners.spotlight: Not solid on his sprint form last year for the Fahey yard and contests another novice race rather than switching to a low-grade handicap; sold for 2,600gns and gelded since last run.
runners.comment: Not solid on his sprint form last year for the Fahey yard

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Now Handicap

runners.name: Thankfully Simmy
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has broadly consistent RPRs; made late headway from rear over 7f at Doncaster on turf debut most recently; possibilities upped further in trip, provided he takes well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Has possibilities upped further in trip, provided he takes to headgear

runners.name: Smoker Bellamy
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard then folded tamely at Beverley (1m2f) last time, failing to back up his reappearance effort (good second over 9.5f on AW); may benefit from this drop back in trip, combined with change of headgear.
runners.comment: May benefit from this drop back in trip, combined with change of headgear

runners.name: Too Darn Spicy
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden who failed to improve when making handicap/turf debut last time but may still be capable of a bigger effort at this sort of level; joint top weight with stablemate Thankfully Simmy.
runners.comment: May still be capable of a bigger effort at this sort of level

runners.name: Mereside Princess
runners.spotlight: Versatile maiden who has made the frame several times at 6f-1m2f, most recently at Ripon; she's a form contender but quite an exposed one who hasn't progressed.
runners.comment: Versatile maiden who is a form contender but quite an exposed one

runners.name: Cobalt Comet
runners.spotlight: All four starts on Southwell AW; belied his 66-1 odds with close third of six in 1m handicap latest; fighting chance provided that form is backed up and transferred to turf.
runners.comment: Close third on handicap debut in latest Southwell AW start; switches to turf

runners.name: Dandy Orton
runners.spotlight: Has achieved meagre RPRs over sprint distances; this new trip needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: New trip needs to make a big difference

runners.name: The Floors Munky
runners.spotlight: Wasn't beaten as far as previously when making handicap debut last time (6f) but achieved another duck egg; form claims are not strong.
runners.comment: Form claims aren't strong

runners.name: Hatamoto
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to a reappearance run that he perhaps needed; his 2yo efforts have received several boosts; interesting on handicap debut, with improvement possible.
runners.comment: Interesting on handicap debut, with improvement possible

runners.name: Helios Path
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced gelding who may show the benefit of this switch to handicap level but is far from solid on hitherto form.
runners.comment: Handicap debutant who is far from solid on form

runners.name: Royal Baroness
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to a triple winner for her connections; achieved little last autumn but may fare better now handicapping; check the market signals for further guidance.
runners.comment: May fare better now handicapping

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Native Instinct
runners.spotlight: Novice winner; 0-8 in handicaps but produced one of his best efforts when 1l second at Hamilton (1m, good) on seasonal debut; possibilities off same mark.
runners.comment: Produced one of his better efforts when 1l second at Hamilton on reappearance

runners.name: Black Storm
runners.spotlight: AW scorer in Ireland; has made the frame in four of his five starts for current yard, gelded prior to fourth at Musselburgh (7f) on reappearance latest; in the mix.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in four of his five starts for current yard; in the mix

runners.name: Vince Le Prince
runners.spotlight: Has form at different tracks but may be ideally suited by Catterick and, back away from that venue, his consistent spell ended last time; no win since 2024.
runners.comment: Has form at different tracks but may be ideally suited by Catterick

runners.name: Green Pursuit
runners.spotlight: Record is only 1-14 but he's attractively treated and proved resurgent with rallying fifth at Thirsk (7f, good) last month on third start for new yard; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Resurgent last time (third start for new yard) and is attractively treated

runners.name: Wish This
runners.spotlight: Recorded sole success in 6f AW event last November; below par kept to that sphere in 2026 and remains inconsistent for current stable; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Recorded sole success in 6f AW event; not the percentage call

runners.name: Detective
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has loads of Carlisle form, including six wins; however, has become inconsistent with age and isn't guaranteed to give his running.
runners.comment: Has loads of Carlisle form but has become inconsistent with age

runners.name: They All Know Me
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who proved consistent until form dipped in last two outings; place claims on earlier efforts but needs to rebound.
runners.comment: Maiden who proved consistent until form dipped in last two starts

runners.name: William Dewhirst
runners.spotlight: Not seen on turf since 2024 but is well treated on best Newcastle AW form and has won over this C&D; admittedly needs to stage a revival judged on 2026 efforts.
runners.comment: Well treated on best Newcastle AW form and has won over this C&D

runners.name: Bay Breeze
runners.spotlight: Multiple 6f winner, mainly on softer than good, most recently off current mark at Carlisle last May; inconsistent since and has something to prove back up in trip.
runners.comment: Multiple 6f winner, including at Carlisle; something to prove back up in trip

runners.name: Judicature
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who has recorded best RPRs over 6f; however, Haydock reappearance effort suggests this return to 7f is worth exploring; could go well.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 4yo who looks worth another crack at this trip; could go well

runners.name: This Farh
runners.spotlight: Drop back to 6f backfired last time when bidding for a Leicester double; has gained all wins at about today's trip, albeit both handicap ones came off 3lb lower.
runners.comment: Has gained all wins at about 7f, albeit both handicap ones came off 3lb lower

races.race_name: racingtv.com EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Kodiak Breeze
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to an Italian 1m 2yo winner; set the pace and made promising debut when clear second at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) 16 days ago; obvious contender.
runners.comment: Set the pace and made promising debut when clear second at Thirsk (5f) 16 days ago

runners.name: Givendale
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to a useful 2yo winner; flashed her tail a couple of times on recent debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft) but looked unlucky not to finish closer than fifth, having been denied a clear run; one to consider.
runners.comment: Unlucky not to finish close than fifth on debut at Beverley; one to consider

runners.name: Wells Fargo Stage
runners.spotlight: 12th foal; half-sister to seven winners including Royal Rascal (6f 2yo including Listed; RPR 100), Cocktail Charlie (5f 2yo; 97), Another Royal (5f-6f; 79) and Penny Royale (5f; 75); dam once-raced (42) half-sister to US 1m Grade 3 winner Tigah; arguably the most interesting of Tim Easterby's squad in this race.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a Listed 2yo winner; arguably the pick of Tim Easterby's squad in this race

runners.name: Arenite
runners.spotlight: 15,000gns yearling; second foal; dam French 6.5f AW winner (RPR 80), half-sister to 6f AW 2yo winner Perfect Sign; makes some appeal on paper; the market may point the way.
runners.comment: Out of a French 6.5f AW winner; makes some appeal on paper; market helpful
runners.owner: G H Thompson And Michael Dods

runners.name: Needin' U
runners.spotlight: 3,000gns yearling; second foal; half-sister to 6f 2yo winner Distant Seas (RPR 95); dam unraced; considered only if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a useful 6f 2yo winner but considered only if lively in the betting

runners.name: Sloequelle
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam placed six times (from 14 runs) in Canada, half-sister to a Canadian 7f stakes winner; from a good stable; no surprise if she's on the premises.
runners.comment: First foal; dam placed six times (from 14 runs) in Canada; from a good stable; a possible
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Tiger In The Tree
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam once-raced (RPR 58) half-sister to a hurdle winner; one of four newcomers from the stable in this race.
runners.comment: First foal from once-raced half-sister to a hurdle winner; stable runs four in this

runners.name: Carry On Chaos
runners.spotlight: 7,000euros yearling; third foal; half-brother to a French 5.5f 2yo winner; dam placed 7f (2yo) and 1m AW (59); one of four newcomers from the stable.
runners.comment: Half-brother to a French 5.5f 2yo winner; stable runs four newcomers in this

runners.name: Dave's Matey
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother to winners Panama Black (1m AW; RPR 81) and Styledome (7f/1m AW 2yo; 76); dam French 7.5f winner, half-sister to Listed-placed 1m/1m2f winner Terre Neuvea and a US 8.5f stakes winner; stable runs four newcomers in this.
runners.comment: Fifth foal from a French 7.5f 2yo winner; stable runs four newcomers in this

runners.name: Formby George
runners.spotlight: £5,000 yearling; second foal; half-brother to a 1m1f winner in KSA; dam 7f-1m1f winner (including 2yo; RPR 79), half-sister to winners Six Strings (6f-8.6f including 2yo) and Chetan (prolific 6f-1m including 2yo); stable has only occasional first-time-out 2yo winners.
runners.comment: £5,000 yearling; bred to be precocious but stable not prolific with 2yo newcomers

runners.name: Peggy's Lad
runners.spotlight: 10,000euros yearling; fourth foal; dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 84), half-sister to winners Cushty (12.4f), The Ginger Queen (US 7.5f/1m) and Szachrajka (Polish 6f-1m1f); stable has modest strike-rate wirh 2yo newcomers.
runners.comment: Out of a useful 7f 2yo winner; would be rare first-time-out 2yo winner for stable

runners.name: Baila Morena
runners.spotlight: Out of a well-connected mare and bred for speed but never really got competitive when 80-1 at Beverley (5f, good to soft) last month.
runners.comment: Never really got competitive when 80-1 at Beverley (5f) last month
runners.owner: T&t Racing Ltd And Keith West

runners.name: Bluestone Lady
runners.spotlight: Showed improvement when second of six at Southwell (5f, AW) 11 days ago, her third start, but that race was run in a slow time and the form looks modest.
runners.comment: Second at Southwell 11 days ago, her third run, but that form looks modest

runners.name: C'Est Lui
runners.spotlight: Out of a 5f 2yo Group 3 winner; started slowly, made mid-race headway and wasn't disgraced when sixth of ten at Bath (5.7f, firm) 19 days ago; needs improvement here, though.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when midfield at Bath (5.7f) but needs improvement here

runners.name: Eighteenth Smiles
runners.spotlight: Has middle-distance pedigree and was just a respectable fifth of ten at Cork (5f, soft) a fortnight ago but is a rare runner here for his much-respected Irish stable, and the extra furlong should be in his favour.
runners.comment: Just a respectable fifth of ten on debut (5f) but represents much-respected Irish stable

runners.name: Crystal Kraken
runners.spotlight: A big-priced outsider for both runs; shaped with promise before fading into seventh at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) a week ago but needs to take another big step forward here.
runners.comment: Showed some promise on her second start but needs to take another big step forward

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Now Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Farndale
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 1m2f-1m4f winner (RPR 75), half-sister to 7f 2yo winner Indy Moon; one of three newcomers from the stable in this race; will probably need longer trips to be seen to best effect.
runners.comment: Newcomer; will probably need longer trips to be seen to best effect

runners.name: Don't Stop Movin
runners.spotlight: 32,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 1m4f-1m6f AW winner (RPR 103), half-sister to winners Dark Island (1m3f) and Eye Knee (1m4f); quite stoutly bred colt; anything he achieves over this trip will be a bonus.
runners.comment: Quite stoutly bred colt; anything he achieves over this trip will be a bonus

runners.name: Hicko's Lad
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at Thirsk (5f, good) on last month's debut and can't be recommended here.
runners.comment: Tailed off at Thirsk (5f, good) on last month's debut and can't be recommended here

runners.name: Mottaret
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 1m4f winner (RPR 66), half-sister to winners Eden Rose (1m6f-2m), Hundred Isles (1m) and Star Blaze (1m); not the obvious type to make a winning debut over 7f.
runners.comment: First foal from a low-grade 1m4f winner; not the obvious type to make a winning debut

runners.name: Revels
runners.spotlight: 350,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 6f/7f winner (including 2yo/Listed; RPR 99), closely related to 7f winner Realised, half-sister to 1m Listed winner Zaman; makes strong appeal on paper.
runners.comment: 350,000gns yearling; first foal from a Listed winner; makes strong appeal on paper

runners.name: Sou'wester
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 6f 2yo winner (RPR 73), half-sister to winners Filibustering (1m6f Flat/hurdle) and Lerwick (1m/8.4f); stable not prolific with 2yo newcomers.
runners.comment: First foal from a fair 6f 2yo winner; stable not prolific with 2yo newcomers

runners.name: Sword Salute
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to 5f winner Mon Na Slieve (including 2yo; RPR 96); dam very useful 7f/1m winner (109), sister to Group-placed 6f 2yo/1m2f winner Artigiano; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Out of a very useful 7f/1m winner; shortlisted on debut
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

races.race_name: Marske Fabrication & Engineering Straight Mile Handicap (Straight Mile Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Up The Jazz
runners.spotlight: Placed twice since dropped back to 1m last month, more recently when very close third (just behind The Sweet Escape) at Leicester (1m, good to firm) nine days ago; competes off same mark here; big player if new cheekpieces have any positive effect.
runners.comment: Very close third off this mark last week; player if cheekpieces have any positive effect

runners.name: Electric Lightning
runners.spotlight: Gained last summer's breakthrough win over 1m and remains unexposed over this trip but finished down the field here (7f) on last month's reappearance and needs that run to have brought him on.
runners.comment: Still unexposed over 1m but needs last month's reappearance run to have brought him on

runners.name: Travis
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; came second at Newcastle (7f, AW) in March and ran well until weakening into fifth at Thirsk (1m, good; refitted cheekpieces) last month but, off current mark and over this trip, his chance is probably reliant on an uncontested lead.
runners.comment: Front-runner; in good form but his chance probably depends upon an uncontested lead

runners.name: We've Got This
runners.spotlight: Scored over 7f here last June and made the frame on her other five outings last season; made low-key reappearance last month but remains unexposed over 1m and has possibilities if boosted by new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Consistent last season; made low-key return but might be boosted by new cheekpieces

runners.name: Thats My Boy Luke
runners.spotlight: Did pretty well to finish fourth on recent seasonal debut at Thirsk (1m, good to firm), given he raced too freely, and he's now back on same mark as when pipping Travis at Ripon (1m, good) last summer.
runners.comment: Ran quite well on seasonal debut and is now back on last summer's winning mark

runners.name: Kiss Me My Love
runners.spotlight: Ran well in defeat at Thirsk (1m, good to firm) this month, despite racing too freely (without cover) in the early stages; remains well handicapped and features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Ran well in defeat at Thirsk last month and remains well handicapped; one to consider

runners.name: Mariner
runners.spotlight: Needed a couple of runs before hitting top gear this season but his close second at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) last month was a good effort and he's still on a workable mark after recent 2lb nudge.
runners.comment: Went close at Doncaster last month and is still on workable mark after 2lb nudge

runners.name: Straight A
runners.spotlight: Consistent during the AW season and posted another good effort when second to progressive rival on recent turf return at Doncaster (7f, good to firm); also suited by 1m and probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Consistent during AW season and posted another good effort on recent turf return

runners.name: Chuzzlewit
runners.spotlight: Regressive 6yo who made a satisfactory reappearance on AW in February but is yet to build on that performance and finished well down the field on last month's turf return; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Regressive 6yo who finished well down the field on last month's turf return

runners.name: Whiskey Pete
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent 5yo; lurks on a dangerous mark but usually runs over middle distances nowadays and was well beaten on last month's seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Usually runs over middle distances nowadays and was well beaten on seasonal debut

runners.name: The Sweet Escape
runners.spotlight: Battled well for narrow win at Nottingham (8.3f, good) last month and probably bettered that performance when very close second at Leicester (1m, good to firm) nine days ago; respected in current form.
runners.comment: Narrow winner at Nottingham in April and recently went very close at Leicester; respected

runners.name: Hachiman
runners.spotlight: Won AW maiden for the Crisford stable last summer but started off for Ruth Carr with three underwhelming AW handicap runs in March/April; revival needed on turf return.
runners.comment: Started off for Ruth Carr with three underwhelming AW runs in March/April
runners.owner: Paul K Spencer And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Thequietman
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; kept on for third in Southwell novice (7f, AW) on stable debut in December but two lesser AW runs followed in January, the latter his handicap debut (1m); returns from break with quite a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Placed on AW on stable debut in December but two lesser AW runs followed in January

races.race_name: 100% Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Ammoony
runners.spotlight: No match for ready winner but kept on well for second on recent handicap debut at Lingfield (1m2f, AW); should be okay back on turf and probably still has more to offer; high on the list.
runners.comment: No match for winner but kept on well for second on handicap debut; has more to offer

runners.name: Fanciulla Del West
runners.spotlight: Could not lay a glove on very promising rival when second of five in Yarmouth novice (11.5f, good to firm) last month but that still goes down as an encouraging seasonal debut, and she should have some improvement to come in handicaps.
runners.comment: No match for exciting rival on seasonal return but can improve now handicapping

runners.name: Bergamo Gold
runners.spotlight: Came good with two AW wins (1m2f/9.4f) in March and made the frame on next two outings but dropped out as though something was amiss at Lingfield last month (race in which Ammoony came second) and probably needs to bounce back with career-best effort on this turf debut.
runners.comment: Dual AW this year; disappointing last time; probably needs a PB on this turf debut

runners.name: Myrrh
runners.spotlight: Won soft-ground Hamilton maiden (8.3f) in the autumn and made quite pleasing seasonal/handicap debut when third in 0-75 contest at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) last month; ought to make her presence felt.
runners.comment: 2yo maiden winner; placed in higher-grade contest on seasonal/handicap debut; player

runners.name: Clocker
runners.spotlight: Unexposed filly who shaped well before fading into fourth in Newcastle maiden (1m, AW) on last month's seasonal debut; upped in trip for this handicap debut; could be a tough nut to crack if that recent outing did her good.
runners.comment: Shaped well in 1m maiden on seasonal return; makes appeal on handicap debut

runners.name: Eze Sur Mer
runners.spotlight: Launched career with two fairly encouraging 9.4f AW runs but finished well down the field at Newbury (1m2f, good) last month and needs improvement on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Began career with two fairly encouraging AW runs; well beaten on turf since

runners.name: Princess Niyla
runners.spotlight: Placed off today's mark at Beverley (8.4f, good to soft) last month and also suited by this C&D but had to settle for fifth when back at Beverley on Tuesday and some of the unexposed 3yos in this line-up look much more interesting; visored last time; usual cheekpieces back on today.
runners.comment: Placed off this mark last month but not quite in same form on Tuesday; others appeal more

runners.name: Letmeseethecolts
runners.spotlight: Took form to new level when drawing clear to score at Pontefract (1m4f, good) in April 2025 but absent since disappointing run over 1m6f a few weeks later, and this drop back to 1m2f is probably not ideal.
runners.comment: Good 1m4f winner in April 2025; absent since running poorly over 1m6f a few weeks later

runners.name: Fire Eyes
runners.spotlight: Held on well to score at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) last month and has no stamina worries over this longer trip but recent 3lb rise is probably enough to leave this 8yo vulnerable to younger rivals here.
runners.comment: Held on well at Doncaster last month but may find younger rivals better handicapped here

races.race_name: Watch Racing TV In Stunning HD Handicap

runners.name: Lumenbourg
runners.spotlight: Generally very consistent over 7f last season but disappointed over that trip on recent seasonal debut and may find 6f here a bit sharp.
runners.comment: Consistent over 7f last season but disappointing on return; may find 6f here too sharp
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho

runners.name: Taygar
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with some below-par efforts but built upon satisfactory reappearance run when runner-up at Ayr (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago and remains 6lb lower than when winning over that C&D last summer.
runners.comment: Second at Ayr this month and still 6lb lower than when winning there last summer

runners.name: Get Up Everybody
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after ten runs but was creditable third from awkward draw on last month's seasonal debut at Pontefract (5f, good to soft; first run after wind surgery) and looks interesting back over 6f.
runners.comment: 0-10 but ran well from awkward draw on seasonal debut at Pontefract (5f); interesting

runners.name: One Of Our Own
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent mare who had edged her way down to a good mark before clearcut C&D (good) win 11 days ago; evidently in good nick but moves up from 0-55 grade today and recent 6lb rise might leave her vulnerable.
runners.comment: Good C&D winner last week but 6lb higher today and moves up from 0-55 grade

runners.name: Jenni
runners.spotlight: Now 5lb lower than when dominating from the front at Musselburgh (7f, good) just over a year ago but slow starts have become an issue in the meantime, and she was always behind when 33-1 for last month's reappearance run; looks risky back at 6f.
runners.comment: 7f winner last spring but slow starts have become a big issue; looks risky back at 6f

runners.name: Sergeant Mayer
runners.spotlight: Best known as a 5f performer but has won over 6f; always behind on last month's reappearance outing and finished last of six after racing too freely in refitted cheekpieces a fortnight later (also 5f); on good mark but needs to get his season up and running.
runners.comment: On good mark but needs to get his season up and running.

runners.name: Ishe Worth Agamble
runners.spotlight: Made good progress last season, collecting three 6f wins along the way; just a respectable third of seven when back on turf last month, after a break, but might come on for the run and remains on a good mark.
runners.comment: Did well last year; just a respectable third last month but might come on for the run

runners.name: Startling
runners.spotlight: 4-21 when trained by David O'Meara, with all the wins gained over 5f on AW; absent since pulled up after early mishap in October and best watched on stable debut.
runners.comment: All four wins for David O'Meara came over 5f on AW; best watched on seasonal/stable debut

runners.name: Profiteer
runners.spotlight: Failed to win last season and absent since 17l defeat in first-time cheekpieces (retained) on AW in November; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Absent since running poorly in new cheekpieces (retained) in November

runners.name: Lindoro
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who showed promise on both starts last summer (6f/7f); made low-key reappearance in 6f AW maiden last month but remains open to improvement on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Made low-key return on AW last month but remains open to improvement on handicap debut

runners.name: Glory Hyde
runners.spotlight: Scored at Wetherby (5.5, good to firm) last month and did well to finish fifth at Beverley (5f, good to firm) on Tuesday, having encountered all sorts of traffic; will be suited by this return to 6f; likely contender.
runners.comment: Scored over 5.5f last month and was unlucky over 5f on Tuesday; 6f also suits; big player

runners.name: Lord Abama
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who made an encouraging seasonal debut in higher-grade contest at Thirsk (6f good; visored) in April; not in same form on either start since (blinkers refitted last time) but has slipped to a tempting mark.
runners.comment: Front-runner; yet to build upon encouraging reappearance but on tempting mark now
runners.owner: Mrs K S Pratt & M Dods

races.race_name: racingtv.com Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Perfect Ruler
runners.spotlight: Out of a Listed-placed 1m AW winner; showed no more than minor promise in her three qualifying races (6f-7f) but represents a top stable and it won't be a surprise if she makes the necessary improvement.
runners.comment: Showed only minor promise in her qualifying races but represents a top stable
runners.owner: A E Oppenheimer And Ms Emily Oppenheimer

runners.name: Full Gas
runners.spotlight: No threat to winner but made good headway from the rear to finish second at Haydock (6f, good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) last month; eased in grade here and looks well worth another crack at 7f; good chance.
runners.comment: Eased in grade after last month's 6f Haydock second; well worth another crack at 7f

runners.name: Harswell River
runners.spotlight: By far the most exposed of these, having raced 18 times, but gave good account when second to progressive rival here (1m, good) 11 days ago and should be okay back at 7f.
runners.comment: Exposed after 18 starts but gave good account when second here (1m) 11 days ago

runners.name: Forever Twenty
runners.spotlight: 0-5 but recovered well from slow start to finish third on recent turf debut at Doncaster (7f, good to firm; first-time tongue-tie) and can be dangerous if she gets away on terms here.
runners.comment: Recovered well from slow start to finish third on recent turf debut; one to consider

runners.name: Twilight Serenade
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on seasonal debut at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) three weeks ago and has now been unplaced all seven starts; needs to have taken significant benefit from that recent outing.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on seasonal debut and has now been unplaced all seven starts

runners.name: Maximum Velocity
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; has a tendency to race too freely and needs to rebound from a disappointing effort last time but his second of 17 at Leicester (7f, good) two starts ago was a good effort; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Didn't fire last time but his second of 17 at Leicester two starts ago was a good effort

runners.name: Go Teejay
runners.spotlight: Made a lot of the running and wasn't beaten far when sixth of nine on recent handicap debut at Doncaster (7f, good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) but needs to find something extra here.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when sixth of nine on recent handicap debut but needs to find extra here

runners.name: Queenies Pal
runners.spotlight: Safely held in both nurseries in October, latterly when third of five at Catterick (6f, soft), and needs to have made some progress during 202-day layoff; cheekpieces removed after one use.
runners.comment: Safely held in two nurseries in October (7f/6f); returns from 202-day layoff

runners.name: Only Dream Big
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only four runs but was unable to land a telling blow when upped to 7f for nursery debut at Newcastle (AW; first-time blinkers) in September and this is her first run since.
runners.comment: No serious threat when upped to 7f for nursery debut in September; first run since

runners.name: Hey Havana
runners.spotlight: 0-5 as a 2yo for Nigel Tinkler but rallied well for fourth on nursery debut at Newcastle (6f, AW) in October and was denied clear run when third over that C&D a few weeks later; upped in trip for stable debut; could have a say if fully tuned up.
runners.comment: Ran well in two 6f AW nurseries in the autumn; upped in trip for seasonal/stable debut

races.race_name: racingtv.com Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Truly Special
runners.spotlight: Well behind Secret Testimony on her final run for David O'Meara last backend before sold for just 900gns; didn't offer a lot on her Wetherby comeback three weeks ago and needs to improve plenty on that.
runners.comment: Didn't offer a lot on her Wetherby comeback for new yard three weeks ago

runners.name: Black Rock Beauty
runners.spotlight: Promised to be suited by the step up to this trip when last seen in February but pulled hard in a messy affair after bumping a rival early; too soon to write him off in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Pulled hard in messy affair after bumping a rival early latest; too soon to write him off

runners.name: Walk On Walk On
runners.spotlight: Picked up from Des Donovan last autumn for 7,750gns following a couple of costly defeats in nurseries; two poor efforts for this yard leave him hard to recommend as he drops from 1m in first-time blinkers (wore cheekpieces on his comeback).
runners.comment: Two poor efforts for this yard leave him hard to recommend as he drops from 1m

runners.name: Enduring Story
runners.spotlight: Big prices and finished a remote last in three runs to date; this is more realistic now handicapping but would want to see some support before considering her.
runners.comment: This more realistic now handicapping but want to see some support before considering her

runners.name: Maybe Soon
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to two AW winners who makes her turf debut back from six weeks off; not much went right on her handicap debut last time, when easy to back, and she's worth a look.
runners.comment: Not much went right on her handicap debut, when easy to back, and she's worth a look

runners.name: Long Shot
runners.spotlight: Only run in sprints to date (dam was a sprinter who's produced sprinters), including three times round here; better effort in first-time cheekpieces on his comeback but failed to build on that in 0-55 company last time.
runners.comment: Better effort here on his comeback but failed to build on that in 0-55 company last time

runners.name: Twilight Dawn
runners.spotlight: String of ordinary efforts last year and another on her comeback a few weeks ago; this is weaker if anything but she's still hard to enthuse about.
runners.comment: String of ordinary efforts last year and another on her comeback a few weeks ago

runners.name: Secret Testimony
runners.spotlight: Handed a 4lb rise for his shock Tapeta winner late last year (only decent effort thus far) and has finished well held in two runs back; will need to improve a deal on last year's turf work but this is easier.
runners.comment: Well held in two runs back; will need to improve a deal on last year's turf work

runners.name: Realistic Dream
runners.spotlight: Regressed in Ireland, is still to finish placed after seven starts (mainly in sprints), and has ducked left leaving the stalls both runs for this yard; not without hope into such a weak handicap down 3lb though.
runners.comment: Most racing in sprints; not without hope into such a weak handicap down 3lb

races.race_name: Sporting Times Handicap

runners.name: Dancing Tiger
runners.spotlight: This looks his optimal trip, having won over it at Doncaster in March, and should be happier back on turf having been unable to take his usual prominent position on the AW a fortnight ago; one of the likelier winners.
runners.comment: Optimal trip and he should be happier back on turf; one of the likelier winners

runners.name: Primo Lara
runners.spotlight: Won his first two handicaps as a 3yo but has regressed for two yards since; held on his AW comeback from wind surgery three weeks ago but is entitled to come on for that and this is weaker in second-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Held on his AW comeback from wind surgery three weeks ago but entitled to come on for that

runners.name: Panama Black
runners.spotlight: Running consistently off his higher AW mark (almost a stone lower on turf), including for new yard five weeks ago; has only gone beyond 1m once before, though, and the longer trip on grass is the double concern.
runners.comment: Better treated than on the AW;  the longer trip on grass is the double concern

runners.name: King's Hand
runners.spotlight: Refused to enter stalls on latest intended run; twice beaten at short prices for new connections,  having been picked up from the Prescott yard for 32,000gns in February; not much wrong with either effort, though, and he's considered on just his second turf start.
runners.comment: Picked up from the Prescott yard for 32,000gns; not much wrong with either effort since

runners.name: Celestias Comet
runners.spotlight: Struggled since winning a 1m Tapeta novice last autumn, latterly returned to that trip on Polytrack without the usual hood (left off again today); something to prove now.
runners.comment: Struggled since winning 1m Tapeta novice last autumn; something to prove now
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Dowling And & Mrs Marney

runners.name: Oman
runners.spotlight: Held off his revised mark since an AW win late last year; not a bad effort back from a break returned to turf at Chester (1m4f; good record first time out in his younger years) last time and this is less competitive.
runners.comment: Not a bad effort back from a break returned to turf last time; this is less competitive

runners.name: Premier
runners.spotlight: Both wins, including one over hurdles, came in a hood, which is absent as he returns from wind surgery after three months off; had plenty of chances off this sort of mark last year.
runners.comment: Both wins came in a hood, which is absent as he returns from wind surgery

runners.name: Bobacious
runners.spotlight: Four wins for two previous yards came from the front, three round undulating courses; finished last season on a downswing, though, and sold for 1,000gns since; there's nothing to suggest he's best caught fresh.
runners.comment: Finished last season on a downswing; sold for 1,000gns since; others more likely

races.race_name: Visit Sri Lanka Handicap

runners.name: Balgowan
runners.spotlight: Has resumed in good form and a 2lb rise for his recent AW win here under Ashley Lewis seems fair; has yet to win on turf (only run on it once since 2023) but otherwise has a decent chance of following up.
runners.comment: 2lb rise for his AW win here under Ashley Lewis seems fair; decent chance of following up

runners.name: High Favour
runners.spotlight: Poor for Jim Bolger as a 2yo and more of the same for this yard back from a break; pulled hard, despite the first-time hood, upped to this trip on AW last time.
runners.comment: Poor for Jim Bolger as a 2yo and more of the same for this yard back from a break

runners.name: Charles Morin
runners.spotlight: Course winner who's finished behind Balgowan on the AW here the last twice; closely matched with his old rival on revised terms and looks set to go well again.
runners.comment: Closely matched with Balgowan on revised terms and looks set to go well again; shortlisted

runners.name: Regally Blonde
runners.spotlight: Had no peace in front on her AW comeback here three weeks ago, when well held at a big price; back to the mark off which she won at Ripon last summer but finished last season on a downswing and needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: No peace in front on her AW comeback here three weeks ago, when well held at a big price
runners.owner: Case Racing Partnership & Anita J Lush

runners.name: Rogue Empire
runners.spotlight: Maiden who's made the running in all four starts since handicapping on Polytrack; dropped 2lb since a fair comeback effort two weeks ago and is entitled to have come on for that.
runners.comment: Dropped 2lb since a fair comeback effort and is entitled to have come on for that

runners.name: Golden Samba
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden who hasn't achieved much in two runs back this year, latterly when a remote fourth of five back on turf.
runners.comment: Lightly raced maiden who hasn't achieved much in two runs back from a break

runners.name: Cherry Cola
runners.spotlight: Early summer has always tended to be her time of year but she's regressed in recent times, is into the veteran stage and entitled to need this after seven months off; others hold more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Regressed in recent times and into the veteran stage; may need this after seven months off

runners.name: Capone
runners.spotlight: Veteran who's been sharply on the downgrade in recent times, coming 22lb down the weights in the past year; a long time since he wore headgear, which is likely fitted more in hope than expectation.
runners.comment: Veteran who's been sharply on the downgrade in recent times, dropped 22lb in past year
runners.owner: Maximum Racing And D Ffrench Davis

runners.name: Smokey Malone
runners.spotlight: Proven winner who can pull hard and did so the last time he went this far (over C&D two summers ago); things didn't pan out his way latest but there's little wiggle room off this mark back on turf after a short break.
runners.comment: Things didn't pan out his way latest but little wiggle room off this mark back on turf

runners.name: Smith
runners.spotlight: Seven wins over 2m on the AW here; getting on and lacks consisency now; hard to recommend having twice been pulled up over hurdles latterly.
runners.comment: Seven wins over 2m on the AW here; getting on and lacks consisency now

runners.name: Lunar Power
runners.spotlight: Won his share in Ireland but has been on the downgrade for a while and has had three different trainers in the past nine months; however, he showed enough on the AW here last time to suggest he has each-way claims back up in trip and down another 2lb.
runners.comment: Showed enough on the AW here last time to suggest he has each-way claims back up in trip

races.race_name: Sporting Times/ Sri Lanka Awaits You EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Notable Dream
runners.spotlight: Had only just turned two at the time of his Doncaster debut this month, when shaping nicely having been held up in a messy affair; the extra furlong will suit both on that evidence and on pedigree; holds obvious claims.
runners.comment: Extra furlong will suit both on debut evidence and on pedigree and he holds obvious claims

runners.name: Encounter
runners.spotlight: 65,000gns foal, 95,000gns yearling who's a brother to Sonic Blue, who won on his 2yo debut (5f), and half-brother to Listed-placed 1m2f AW course winner Ermesinde; his trainer's first 2yo runner of the year and Ryan Moore's only ride of the day.
runners.comment: 95,000gns yearling; trainer's first 2yo runner of the year/jockey's only ride of the day

runners.name: Rafe's Da Man
runners.spotlight: 65,000gns yearling; half-brother to 3yo debutant winner (1m, Tapeta) Nordic Norm; holds a sales-race entry in the autumn and the market will be a useful guide.
runners.comment: 65,000gns yearling; holds a sales-race entry in the autumn; market will be a useful guide

runners.name: Dashing Digger
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns yearling whose dam progressed sharply with time and turned out useful at around 1m2f; trainer is 5-66 with 2yos in recent seasons (0-5 here) and this is his first juvenile runner of the season.
runners.comment: 10,000gns yearling whose dam progressed sharply with time; likely best watched

runners.name: Theheatison
runners.spotlight: £18,000 breeze-up 2yo; dam (produced a 6f winner in Italy) won her first two starts in sprints as a 2yo for Richard Hannon; worth a market check for trainer who's done well with jumpers since taking out a licence.
runners.comment: £18,000 breeze-up 2yo; worth market check for trainer who's doing well with jumpers

runners.name: Bin Waary
runners.spotlight: 62,000gns yearling; may well have learned from his Polytrack debut over 6f at Kempton, when always trapped wide from the outside stall; ought to do better.
runners.comment: Ought to have learned from AW debut (6f) when always trapped wide from the outside stall

races.race_name: Sporting Times/Sri Lanka Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Behike
runners.spotlight: 500,000gns yearling; well backed ahead of his 7f debut (soft) last backend, when inclined to pull hard and not seeing it out behind a useful rival; built on that on his 6f AW comeback here, when impressive from the front; plenty of potential and holds a Group 1 entry.
runners.comment: Group 1 entrant; impressive from the front on his AW comeback here; plenty of potential

runners.name: Ray Mon Dough
runners.spotlight: Easily beat his main market rival (subsequent winner) on AW debut here last autumn; inclined to pull hard and said to have hung left when well beaten at a short price on his 7f Kempton comeback; will be happier back sprinting on his turf debut.
runners.comment: Hung left when well beaten at short price on 7f comeback; will be happier back sprinting

runners.name: Railwayman
runners.spotlight: 420,000gns yearling; half-brother to Group 1 winner Oxted; one of four to come away on his debut last month and built on that when winning at Ayr (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; should have more to offer but needs improvement under a penalty.
runners.comment: Won at Ayr (6f, good to firm) this month; should have more to offer but needs improvement
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Jean's Boy
runners.spotlight: Gelded £60,000 yearling who's a half-brother to useful 3yo sprinter Leodis Dream and a couple of lesser winners; would need to be above average if he's to make a successful debut.
runners.comment: Half-brother to useful 3yo sprinter Leodis Dream; would need to be above average to win

runners.name: Rianka
runners.spotlight: Third foal of a mare who won in France at up to 9.5f; 66-1, finished well held on Tapeta debut and she's probably one for handicaps down the line.
runners.comment: 66-1, finished well held on her Tapeta debut; probably one for handicaps down the line

runners.name: Wayward Queen
runners.spotlight: Well held in two starts this spring and would need a massive amount of improvement to get involved; will likely find life easier once handicapping after today.
runners.comment: Well held in two starts and will likely find life easier once handicapping after today

races.race_name: Sri Lanka Wonder Of Asia Handicap

runners.name: Profit Street
runners.spotlight: Plenty of short-priced defeats to go with four AW wins (three here over 7f) in recent times for James Owen, from whom he was picked up for 5,000gns recently; fine on turf and has a handy draw.
runners.comment: Picked up for 5,000gns recently; consistent, fine on turf and has a handy draw

runners.name: Invincible Speed
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner, including twice here, who looked to find a stiffer 6f enough of a test back on turf last time; consistent and is one to consider in a race that may set up for him.
runners.comment: Four-time AW winner; consistent and is one to consider in a race that may set up for him

runners.name: Spendmore Lane
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who's had plenty of moments at the stalls; got it right when registering her third AW win of the year last time, but returns to turf on a career-high mark upped 4lb and the extra furlong is a concern.
runners.comment: Front-runner who's had plenty of moments at the stalls; the extra furlong is a concern

runners.name: Recon Mission
runners.spotlight: Has run well fresh in the past but is knocking on and may need this after eight months off; doesn't often go beyond 5f these days and is drawn low, so has a bit to overcome.
runners.comment: May need this after eight months off; doesn't often go beyond 5f these days

runners.name: Travel Agent
runners.spotlight: Has got it together for his current (third) yard since fitted with an eyeshield; needs to improve on previous turf efforts but a 3lb rise looks fair and he has a decent chance of completing the hat-trick.
runners.comment: Got it together for his current (third) yard since fitted with an eyeshield; decent claims

runners.name: No Return
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding who's proved inconsistent for this yard having missed six months; handicapper easing off now but he again didn't offer a huge amount back on turf last month.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish gelding who's proved inconsistent for this yard having missed six months

runners.name: Hedge Fund
runners.spotlight: Useful 2yo who's been tough to place since, proving patchy for this yard (which won this 12 months ago) during a limited campaign last year; pretty much everything went wrong on his comeback and he's entitled to improve for that down 3lb.
runners.comment: Everything went wrong on his comeback and he's entitled to improve for that down 3lb

runners.name: Twilight Jet
runners.spotlight: Useful in Ireland in his younger days but has come 22lb down the weights in the space of a year for this yard; capable of popping up at this lower level but others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Come 22lb down the weights in the space of a year for this yard; just each-way claims

runners.name: Whenthedealinsdone
runners.spotlight: Landed a hat-trick on the AW early last year, the final leg off 2lb lower; back in form lately, most recently in a race that didn't pan out his way, but needs a bit more back on turf from the lowest stall (likely to be dropped in again).
runners.comment: Needs a bit more back on turf from the lowest stall (likely to be dropped in again)

races.race_name: Sporting Times Sprint Handicap

runners.name: Tie Fighter
runners.spotlight: Yard enjoying a good run and he made a fair comeback from a wide stall on the AW (6f) a fortnight ago; again edged right there, so stall 9 ought to work in his favour in first-time headgear, and he's one to consider now handicapping.
runners.comment: Yard enjoying a good run and he made a fair comeback; one to consider now handicapping

runners.name: Viking Glory
runners.spotlight: A few costly defeats for Adrian Keatley at Newcastle in the winter (6f-1m); fluffed the start for his new yard on handicap debut at Windsor a fortnight ago and sports a first-time hood on this first crack at 5f.
runners.comment: Blew the start for his new yard on handicap debut; sports a hood on this first crack at 5f

runners.name: Solar Invincible
runners.spotlight: Improver since cheekpieces went on and possible he found firm ground against him upped in class last time; also possible he's reached his ceiling mark now and he'll need more from the lowest stall.
runners.comment: Improver since cheekpieces went on; possible he's reached his ceiling mark now

runners.name: Beach Partee
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner for William Knight last autumn, latterly off this mark, who's struggled in two runs for his current yard this spring; needs a revival.
runners.comment: Struggled in two runs for his current yard this spring; needs a revival

runners.name: Adalida
runners.spotlight: Maiden who's been expensive to follow, including in a couple of handicaps since returned to turf; has often been inclined to pull hard and is dropping to 5f for the first time, which ought to help.
runners.comment: Often inclined to pull hard and is dropping to 5f for the first time, which ought to help

runners.name: Red Snapper
runners.spotlight: Cheekpieces have helped and he just looked to find Ascot's 6f stretching him in better company last time; whether today's easy 5f is too far the other way is the question.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces have helped; today's easy 5f may be on the sharp side down from 6f

runners.name: Enter Sandman
runners.spotlight: Progressed on the AW this spring since fitted with a tongue-tie and cheekpieces, coming away with the favourite last time; has been ridden forward latterly, so stall 2 doesn't look ideal upped another 4lb, and he needs to transfer the improvement to turf.
runners.comment: Progressed on the AW this spring; needs to transfer the improvement to turf upped 4lb

runners.name: Life After Love
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-3 over Southwell's 5f last time, despite again edging to her left; successful siblings did their winning on the AW too and she'll need more again upped 4lb on her turf debut.
runners.comment: Made it 3-3 over Southwell's 5f last time; needs more again upped 4lb on her turf debut

runners.name: Yes Waliim
runners.spotlight: A few costly defeats to date, twice hanging left on Tapeta last year; the money came again on his handicap debut a fortnight ago, having undergone wind surgery, and he's evidently felt capable of better; drops from 6f under 7lb claimer Oliver Hoodless (1-7).
runners.comment: A few costly defeats to date; evidently felt capable of better; drops from 6f today

runners.name: Bessie Wallis
runners.spotlight: Found a weak AW novice when winning here last autumn and has finished well held in two handicaps since; had excuses each time, pulling hard over 6f on her comeback, but she has to prove this mark within range on her second turf start.
runners.comment: Found a weak AW novice here last autumn; has to prove this mark within range

races.race_name: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Concert Pitch
runners.spotlight: Has quite a lot of stamina in his pedigree but he's placed in two 5f runs at Beverley (good/good to soft) and he took a major step forward with his close second two weeks ago; that form puts him firmly in the picture and he's open to more progress on this step up in trip.
runners.comment: Close second at Beverley latest and he's open to more progress upped in trip

runners.name: May Bee In Profit
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much on Beverley debut but she found lots of improvement when a close second there (5f, good to soft) two weeks ago; that was at 25-1 but she pulled clear of the third and sets the standard on that form; key player.
runners.comment: Big improvement when runner-up at Beverley and she sets the standard on that form

runners.name: Top Cote
runners.spotlight: Finished well for a close second on Leicester debut (5f, good to firm) and he ran to a similar level when third of ten at Bath (Terry O fourth) last time; should go well again but he needs improvement to make a serious impact at this new trip; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Placed in both his runs but he needs improvement at this new trip; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Serenity Bay
runners.spotlight: April foal; 10,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Fast Fred (1m2f-12.4f; RPR 82) and Saxon Way (Polish 1m3f); dam 1m4f AW winner (82), half-sister to useful winners Crossford (7f/1m including 2yo) and So Suave (7f-8.5f including 2yo); has decent standard to aim at on debut and she could be a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: Has decent standard to aim at on debut and she could be a longer-term prospect

runners.name: Velma Dinkley
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns foal; second foal; dam 1m winner (RPR 78), half-sister to 7f winner Attentive, out of French 12.5f winning half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Visionario; could be one for later on and she's probably best watched on debut.
runners.comment: 1,000gns foal; could be one for later on and she's probably best watched on debut

runners.name: Better Nature
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unplaced 6f/7f (RPR 48), half-sister to winners Awesometank (1m Listed), Frantanck (1m) and Modern Approach (9.4f), out of useful 6f 2yo winner, is granddaughter of 5f/6f Listed winner; already gelded and this looks a tough starting point.
runners.comment: Already gelded and this looks a tough starting point

runners.name: Celtic Charioteer
runners.spotlight: January foal by Mondialiste; fifth foal; brother to UAE 9.5f dirt winner Withering (RPR 109); dam unraced sister to 5.5f-1m winner Predetermined, half-sister to winners Classic Blade (6f 2yo Group 2), Raadobarg (smart 7f-1m) and Captain Dunne (smart 5f); yard has had only one 2yo runner this season but that was a winner at Newbury on Friday.
runners.comment: Yard has had only one 2yo runner this season but that was winner at Newbury on Friday

runners.name: Tina Fromtransport
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when third at Bath (5.7f, firm) last month but she was beaten around 6l and that form is well below the standard here; others preferred.
runners.comment: Some ability when third at Bath but that form is well below the standard here

runners.name: Terry O
runners.spotlight: Beaten around 4l on Bath debut (5f, good) two weeks ago but that doesn't tell the whole story because he was short of room and became unbalanced after hitting the rail at a vital stage; did well to snatch fourth in the end and he's a likely improver on this step up in trip.
runners.comment: Ran better than the bare facts at Bath and he's a likely improver upped in trip

runners.name: Fire Thunder
runners.spotlight: Ran green after a slow start on Wolverhampton debut (6f, AW) but she got hang of things in the closing stages and was only narrowly denied by a runner with experience; should be more streetwise this time and she's a player if she can build on that eyecatching debut.
runners.comment: Finished well to go close at 25-1 on Wolverhampton debut (6f, AW); respected

runners.name: Graceful George
runners.spotlight: Rallied well to finish a 2l-fourth at a biggish price on Kempton debut (6f, AW) 18 days ago; should benefit from that experience but this looks a tougher race and he needs a big step forward.
runners.comment: Showed promise at Kempton but he needs plenty of progress on this switch to turf

runners.name: Kach Above
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise when fourth of 11 at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) last Monday but that was an ordinary race and he needs plenty of progress on this switch to turf.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise on Wolverhampton debut last week but that was an ordinary race

races.race_name: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Fitzdares Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Rhythm N Hooves
runners.spotlight: Won three 5f handicaps in 2025; continued to run well on his first three starts this year but he didn't really fire behind a stablemate at Goodwood (5f, good) last time; that took his losing run up to ten and he needs to get back near his best.
runners.comment: Generally reliable 6yo but he wasn't at his best at Goodwood latest; needs to find more

runners.name: Regal Envoy
runners.spotlight: 7yo who recorded his 13th win when making all off a reduced mark at Bath (5f, good) two weeks ago; 3lb higher on this step back up in grade but he remains well treated on his best form and is 2-2 over C&D, including a success off current mark in this race 12 months ago; key player.
runners.comment: 13-time winner who made all at Bath latest and is 2-2 over C&D; key player

runners.name: All Ways Glamorous
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who reappeared with a creditable third of 17 at Ascot (5f, good to firm) 17 days ago; ran right up to his best with that bold bid and he's respected off unchanged mark; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: C&D winner who reappeared with a good third of 17 at Ascot; respected off unchanged mark

runners.name: Redorange
runners.spotlight: Won at Chester (5f, good) last May and he's run some good races in big fields since including at York four days ago; finished eighth of 21 in that race but he didn't get much luck and that was an eye-catching effort after 235 days off; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Didn't get much luck when an eye-catcher on York reappearance four days ago; interesting

runners.name: Rogue Enforcer
runners.spotlight: Dual 5f winner as a 2yo for Peter Chapple-Hyam and he also scored three times in France/Italy last year; raised his form to a new level in the second half of last season but he failed to beat a rival on stable debut at Goodwood 17 days ago; comes with risks attached after that and he needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Three wins in France/Italy last season but was last of 11 on recent stable debut

runners.name: Adrestia
runners.spotlight: Won the Palace Of Holyroodhouse Handicap at Royal Ascot (5f) last June and hard to knock her subsequent defeats including a near-miss at Glorious Goodwood; returns after 239 days off but she still looks feasibly treated and was a good third over C&D on her reappearance last season; dangerous.
runners.comment: Won handicap at Royal Ascot last term; still has low mileage and she's respected on return

runners.name: Marty Hopkirk
runners.spotlight: Has good chance on last summer's Doncaster second but he was well held in his next three runs; still lightly raced but he's been up and down so far and others are more solid.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo but he's been up and down so far

runners.name: Democracy Dilemma
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who looks well handicapped on his win at Haydock (5f, good) last autumn but he's been disappointing at Musselburgh and Goodwood this spring; has some questions to answer and he needs a major revival with headgear removed; stablemate of Rhythm N Hooves.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but he needs a major revival with headgear removed; one of two for yard

runners.name: Baldomero
runners.spotlight: His turf wins have come on good or slower including heavy ground; three wins during his latest spell on AW but he's been well held back on turf in last two runs; bit to prove again but he's now 2lb lower than for his last success and has claims if he can get back on track; change of headgear.
runners.comment: Well held back on turf in last two runs but he's now 2lb lower than for last success

runners.name: Desert Cop
runners.spotlight: His only win in two years came in Bahrain last March; returned to Britain with respectable efforts at Nottingham (5f) and Windsor (6f) this spring but he was laboured at Newbury on Saturday; this is another quick turnaround and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Just one win since May 2024 and was laboured at Newbury on Saturday; down the list

runners.name: Gaeli
runners.spotlight: Added to his five turf wins in Italy when scoring on AW/stable debut at Southwell (5f) in March; hit a personal best with that comfortable success but he was only sixth off this revised mark at Southwell last time; needs to step up again after that and others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Ex-Italian; won on stable debut at Southwell but he was down the field there last time

races.race_name: Tattersalls £40,000 EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Chimes Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Finished down the field on Salisbury debut (7f, good) last autumn but she reappeared with a close call over this trip at Haydock (good to firm) last month; rallied well for a clear second in that race and that was a promising effort behind a 1-2 shot; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Reappeared with close call at Haydock last month and that was behind a 1-2 shot; respected

runners.name: Tahalel
runners.spotlight: Struggled in the Albany (Group 3) last summer but she's been placed in her other three runs, including a close third on her reappearance at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) last month; went down fighting in that race and she's respected on this step up to 1m.
runners.comment: Placed in three of her four runs including at Doncaster last month; respected upped to 1m

runners.name: Withtearsinmyeyes
runners.spotlight: RPRs in the low 80s when a clear second at Nottingham (8.3f, good) and Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) this spring; sets the standard back on turf and this well-bred filly should have more to offer; strongly respected.
runners.comment: RPRs in low 80s when a clear second in her two runs this spring; leading contender

runners.name: Trelissick
runners.spotlight: Kingman filly who made an encouraging start when fourth at Kempton (7f, AW) last month and that was behind a useful rival; should improve for that experience and it wouldn't be a surprise if she was a factor on this step up in trip.
runners.comment: Kingman filly who made an encouraging start at Kempton last month; could be in the mix

runners.name: Quisana
runners.spotlight: First living foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Querari (Italian 1m2f Group 1), Quidura (US/Canadian 8.5f/1m1f Grade 2) and Quasillo (German 1m2f Group 3), out of useful 1m2f/10.5f winner; has good standard to aim at on debut but she needs watching in market.
runners.comment: Well-bred filly and she needs watching in market on debut

runners.name: Zeriya
runners.spotlight: 480,000gns yearling; well backed into 1-2 at Southwell (1m, AW) last month but she was always behind after an awkward start and finished last of five; should know much more this time but she needs to leave that debut form a long way behind.
runners.comment: Finished last when odds-on at Southwell and needs to leave that form a long way behind

runners.name: Cap Santa Lucia
runners.spotlight: Attracted support at big prices at Newbury (7f, good) last month but she faded into a well-held sixth of 11; should benefit from that experience but she needs plenty of improvement upped in trip.
