runners.comment: Well-held sixth at a biggish price on Newbury debut (7f) last month; others preferred

runners.name: Madame Passant
runners.spotlight: Big prices and she's struggled in two 1m events on Polytrack and turf this spring.
runners.comment: Big prices and she's struggled in two 1m events on Polytrack and turf this spring

runners.name: Rajwaah
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly but she finished out the back in two runs at Kempton (7f/1m) last month and was 40-1 last time; still early days but she remains best watched for now.
runners.comment: Finished out the back in two runs at Kempton (7f/1m) last month and was 40-1 last time

runners.name: Moonlit Surf
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to 1m2f winner Wave Rider (RPR 101); second-favourite on Kempton debut (7f, AW) in October but she weakened before finishing a well-held sixth of nine; should improve with time and distance but she has plenty to find on her return.
runners.comment: Well held in sole 2yo run and needs major improvement upped in trip on her return

races.race_name: Track Radio On Digital & DAB Handicap

runners.name: Sandscreendeliverd
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-13 but he's been knocking on the door at Lingfield (6f, AW) and Goodwood (5f, good) this spring; up another 2lb but this strong traveller was only just caught last time and the first three pulled clear in that race; key player.
runners.comment: Has been knocking on the door this spring and he should make another bold bid; key player

runners.name: Kiss And Run
runners.spotlight: C&D winner but she's on a long losing run and was beaten 9l on Goodwood reappearance (6f, good) 17 days ago; that was a Class 3 but she needs to leave that form a long way behind on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: On long losing run and was beaten 9l on her Goodwood reappearance; opposable

runners.name: Maharajas Express
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in this race last year and he scored off 1lb lower at Chester (5f, good) last July; never a threat on his reappearance at Lingfield (6f) last month but he pulled hard and this drop back in trip is a positive; usually comes from off the pace and has claims if he gets the breaks.
runners.comment: Hold-up performer who was second in this race last year; dangerous if he gets the breaks

runners.name: The Flying Seagull
runners.spotlight: Most of his turf form is on good/good to firm; looks interesting on his best efforts last season but he was disappointing at Wolverhampton in March and sole win was in a maiden in June 2024; others preferred.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but was disappointing on AW latest and sole win was in June 2024
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Rogue Bullet
runners.spotlight: Won off 1lb lower over C&D (good to firm) last June but he's had only three runs since and has been well held in all of them; has something to prove and he probably wouldn't want any rain.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last June but well held in all three subsequent runs; down the list

runners.name: Al Barez
runners.spotlight: His last win was off 15lb higher but that was on AW in April 2024 and he has mixed record in four runs this year; ran well at Kempton (6f) in March but he was remote when third at Leicester last time and needs to bounce back again after that disappointing effort.
runners.comment: 7yo who is not easy to predict and his last win was in April 2024; down the list

runners.name: General Assembly
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; two AW wins (5f) last autumn but he returned after a short break with a well-held fifth of eight at Pontefract (5f, good) last month; may have needed that run but his sole turf win was two years ago and others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: On workable mark but his sole turf win was two years ago and others are more persuasive

runners.name: Lequinto
runners.spotlight: Versatile trip-wise and six of his 12 wins have come at Windsor; on same mark as for his Sandown win last autumn and he's been running well on AW this spring; should get a good tow into this race and he's in the mix back at this track.
runners.comment: Six-time course winner who has been running well on AW this spring; in the mix

runners.name: Sarafina Mshairi
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been on Tapeta and latest was at Southwell (6f) in her final run last season; 4lb higher on her return but she's still lightly raced on turf and should be unfazed by this drop back in trip; needs watching in market.
runners.comment: Dual Tapeta winner who is still lightly raced on turf; needs watching in market on return

runners.name: Shavkat
runners.spotlight: 14-1 winner on Lingfield debut (6f, AW) for Roger Varian last June; beaten around 4l in two subsequent runs, including on stable debut in March, but it's still early days and he looks on a workable mark in his first handicap; needs watching in market on this drop in trip.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer who looks feasibly treated on best novice form; one to keep an eye on

runners.name: Jax Edge
runners.spotlight: Six-time sprint winner who handles any ground; has faded in both runs this season but she's well treated on her best form last year including a front-running win at Windsor last summer; one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Handles most ground and she's well treated if she can get back near best; not ruled out

runners.name: Isle Of Lismore
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm and heavy; on reduced mark and was a close third at Lingfield (5f, good to soft; first-time visor) last July but he's been on the sidelines since; has bit to prove after a lengthy absence and this is a competitive race; visor removed and cheekpieces are reapplied.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but he returns after a lengthy absence and this is competitive

races.race_name: Fitzdares Telephone & Text Betting Handicap

runners.name: Grey Sands
runners.spotlight: Easy winner in a Southwell handicap (1m, AW) for Joseph O'Brien in January; changed hands for 28,000gns after that but he came up short when favourite on stable debut at Lingfield (1m2f) last month; still has potential after only six runs but he needs to find more at this new trip.
runners.comment: Still has potential after only six runs but he needs to find more at this new trip

runners.name: King Of Berkshire
runners.spotlight: Camelot colt who went close on handicap debut at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to firm) last month before making virtually all in similar conditions at Redcar nine days later; this is tougher off 6lb higher but he pulled clear with the runner-up last time and he's still unexposed; respected.
runners.comment: Made the breakthrough when making all at Redcar; respected off 6lb higher

runners.name: Any Which Way
runners.spotlight: Well held at big prices in three 2yo runs but he looked a different proposition when justifying favouritism on handicap debut at Nottingham (1m2f, good) last month; up 6lb but that was a comfortable success and he's open to more progress at this new trip; big player for yard that won this in 2021 and 2022.
runners.comment: Justified favouritism on handicap debut at Nottingham; big player again off 6lb higher

runners.name: Morbeh
runners.spotlight: Well-related gelding who has done well since switched to handicaps, winning on AW at Lingfield (1m2f) in January and going close at Goodwood (1m2f, good) 17 days ago; only 1lb higher than for that last run and he's an interesting contender at this new trip; cheekpieces worn last time are removed.
runners.comment: Has done well since switched to handicaps and he's open to more progress at this new trip

runners.name: Oh Yes You Do
runners.spotlight: Won a Leicester maiden (1m, good to soft) in October and she found more progress when runner-up on handicap debut at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) last month; 2lb higher at this new trip but she kept on well last time and pulled clear of the third; in the mix.
runners.comment: Runner-up on handicap debut at Doncaster (1m2f) last month; in the mix at new trip

runners.name: Sir Griflet
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in his first two starts but he showed ability when fifth in a Salisbury maiden (1m4f, good to firm) 15 days ago; should have more to offer but this is competitive on handicap debut and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Unexposed gelding but this looks is competitive on handicap debut and others are preferred

runners.name: Wardlaw
runners.spotlight: Beaten 10l or more in three 2yo runs (7f/1m) but his RPRs were progressive and this is more realistic on handicap debut; has been gelded and he needs a close look upped to this trip on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Has been gelded and he needs a close look upped to this trip on handicap/seasonal debut

races.race_name: Find Us At fitzdares.com Handicap (GBBplus Race)

runners.name: Charlie's Choice
runners.spotlight: All eight wins have been on AW but he's lost his way with heavy defeats at Newcastle (12.4f, Tapeta) and Lingfield (2m, Polytrack) in last two runs; still lightly raced on turf but he needs a major revival back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Eight-time AW winner but he's lost his way in last two runs; lots to prove back on turf

runners.name: Sir William
runners.spotlight: Won here (1m2f, good) for William Haggas last August but that was off 4lb lower and he's struggled in two runs for new yard this season; has plenty to prove and overall record is now 1-15.
runners.comment: Has struggled in both runs for new yard this season and overall record is now 1-15

runners.name: Siouxfonic
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who landed a Wolverhampton maiden (1m4f) in December but he's been well held in two turf handicaps this spring; ran into some trouble at Chester (12.3f, good) ten days ago but he needs to find more to defy this mark; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo but he's been well held in both handicaps and needs to find more

runners.name: Wisper
runners.spotlight: All seven wins have been on turf and latest was over C&D last June; didn't fire in her final two runs last season but this is a drop back in grade and she won on her seasonal return in 2022 and 2023; needs a close look off dangerous mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner who is on dangerous mark and has good record when fresh; needs close look

runners.name: Don Simon
runners.spotlight: Returned from a break with two creditable efforts on Tapeta (1m4f/2m) this spring but his form slumped back on turf at Thirsk (1m4f, good) last month; has something to prove again after that and he's still a maiden after 11 starts.
runners.comment: Record of 0-11 and his form slumped back on turf at Thirsk (1m4f) last time

runners.name: Son Of Man
runners.spotlight: Just two wins from 25 starts but latest was at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) last month and he had a near-miss on his return visit three weeks ago; up another 3lb but he remains feasibly treated and his first win was on turf; strongly respected.
runners.comment: 5yo who has had a win and near-miss on AW in last two starts; respected back on turf

runners.name: Due To Henry
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm and soft; all three wins have been on turf and he returned to form after a break with a near-miss at Haydock (10.4f) last month; up 3lb but he pulled clear of the third last time and is a big player if he can back that up.
runners.comment: Turned things around after a break with a near-miss at Haydock last month; shortlisted

runners.name: Naval Command
runners.spotlight: Improved with each of his three qualifying runs, culminating in a clear second behind a useful rival in Newmarket novice (1m, good to firm) last June; left Charlie Appleby for 80,000gns since; returns after almost a year off but he's bred to stay this new trip and is open to more progress on handicap/stable debut; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Progressive form in qualifying runs and he looks interesting on handicap/stable debut

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Marra Donna
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but involved in close finishes over 7f and 6f (AW) in February and only just failed to get up at Lingfield (5f) in March; no serious impact at Southwell since but ran as though still in form and not ruled out if able to transfer his sand form back to turf (first run on grass since debut).
runners.comment: 0-8 but involved in close finishes at 7f, 6f and 5f on AW; chance if as good back on turf

runners.name: Corniche Girl
runners.spotlight: One win from 16 starts, over 6f on Kempton AW last August; in the frame on sand six times since but she hardly shifts in the weights; probably handles turf but needs a bit more for the return to this surface if she's to get her head in front again.
runners.comment: 1-16, over 6f on AW last August; many frame efforts since; needs a bit more back on turf
runners.owner: C Munro, Mrs M Gander And Partner

runners.name: Zoulette
runners.spotlight: Finished remote behind two good 3yos on Lingfield AW (6f) last month but previously showed evidence of ability when third of five at Wolverhampton (5f) in March, keeping on well; should be more at home in ordinary handicaps; considered on turf debut.
runners.comment: Stiff task latest; evidence of ability over 5f in March; lowly mark now handicapping

runners.name: Arvana Belle
runners.spotlight: Successful handicap debut at Brighton (6f, good) in August; that wasn't competitive but she ran well on her final 2yo starts there and 1m stretched her at Bath on comeback; shouldn't rule out back at 6f.
runners.comment: Winning handicap debut over 6f; close up final 2yo starts; 1m stretched her on return

runners.name: Yachtsman
runners.spotlight: Faded over 5f here on handicap debut and final start for the Gosdens; ran well when a staying-on fourth on Lingfield AW (7f) in March on second start for this yard but dropped right out at Leicester since (also 7f; hood refitted, now discarded); suspicion for now that the AW may suit best.
runners.comment: Promising close 4th on AW (7f) in March; well held back on turf since; surface query

runners.name: Rosieisme Darling
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins on Polytrack and mostly kept to that trip, seeming not to see out the extra furlong in three handicaps at 6f, most recently at Wolverhampton this month; others may well find more again.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins on Polytrack but has been a weak finisher in all 3 handicaps over this trip

runners.name: Madman
runners.spotlight: Kept busy by previous yard, getting off the mark on Kempton AW (6f) in January; inevitably more exposed than some but he acts on turf and ran well for a long way over C&D this month for his new yard; a likely contender.
runners.comment: Kept busy; off the mark on AW (6f) in January; creditable 6th for new yard here latest

runners.name: Chelio
runners.spotlight: Improved with each 2yo run but his second of eight on Lingfield AW (1m) in December came in an ordinary novice; entitled to need the run on handicap/turf debut at Leicester (7f, good to firm) and he ran respectably in sixth, eased when beaten; may be capable of better over the new trip.
runners.comment: Improved with each 2yo run; respectable reappearance on handicap/turf debut; new trip

runners.name: Cape Toronada
runners.spotlight: Limited encouragement when qualifying for this level and on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (about 1m, AW); however, she came home with a wet sail to take a close fourth at Bath (extended 5f, good) this month and things do seem to be falling into place now; likely contender.
runners.comment: Modest form in first 4 starts but finished strongly to take a close 4th (about 5f) latest

runners.name: My Dad Tom
runners.spotlight: Maiden; progress stalled on AW but 7f was too far for him in March and he can't be ruled out on his most recent turf run, when finishing strongly to be second of nine at Yarmouth (6f, good) in October; possibilities.
runners.comment: Progress stalled on AW; chance on staying-on 2nd on latest turf run (6f) in October

runners.name: Stolen
runners.spotlight: No threat in three 6f runs but she would have finished closer to a subsequent winner at Lingfield (6f, AW) in April had her jockey not had to concentrate on trying to prevent her hanging; found little over C&D since; should fare better now handicapping but new cheekpieces are an unknown.
runners.comment: Signs of ability when qualifying over 6f; open to improvement now handicapping

runners.name: Nzuri
runners.spotlight: Modest form on first two starts but encouraging to see the way she ran from the front at Kempton (6f, AW) recently, when no match for a Haggas improver but punched on to regain second; of interest now realistically placed in an ordinary handicap.
runners.comment: Modest on first 2 starts; creditable 2nd behind an improver on AW since; handicap debut

races.race_name: Roscommon Claiming Maiden

runners.name: Star Of Beauty
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far when third of 13 in a Naas maiden (6f, good) last August; two maiden runs this year below that level; a doubt about her official rating of 75 was raised by her handicap debut at the Curragh, when last of 12; on the plus side, the stable has had two recent winners.
runners.comment: Doubt raised by a poor handicap run at the Curragh, fair chance based on best form at two
runners.owner: David K Kelly & Mrs Pauline Condon

runners.name: Mo Anam Chara
runners.spotlight: Two Dundalk runs last November/December were much better than previous turf efforts on soft ground; faded in the final furlong on handicap debut in a 1m contest at the Curragh a month ago; this shorter trip might suit her better.
runners.comment: By far her best form in two AW runs, faded in a 1m turf handicap last time, 7f may suit

runners.name: Cosmic Funk
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Gowran on his first two outings; a bit better when fifth of nine at Leopardstown eight days again; Colin Keane aboard for the first time and can feature in a weak race.
runners.comment: Leopardstown fifth was a small step in the right direction, can feature in a weak race

runners.name: Monamie
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; Cotai Glory filly out of a Galileo mare who was a 1m4f winner for John Oxx; dam closely related to useful hurdle/chase winner Glenstal Abbey, half-sister to Group-placed 7f/1m winner Margay; potentially interesting in this grade on debut.
runners.comment: By Cotai Glory out of a Galileo mare who was a 1m4f winner for John Oxx, good pedigree

runners.name: Prince Andorra
runners.spotlight: Cheap purchase but outran 150-1 odds when seventh of 14 on 1m Dundalk debut, beaten around 7l after a slow break; appeared not to stay 1m at Bellewstown.
runners.comment: Ran well at a huge price on debut at Dundalk, appeared not to stay 1m at Bellewstown

runners.name: Due In June
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister to a winner at around 7f; dam a maiden, half-sister to Italian 5f 2yo Listed winner Lorelei Rock; stable's Flat winners typically come with older horses.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a winner at around 7f, stable's Flat wins typically with older horses

runners.name: Fay Og
runners.spotlight: Has finished in rear in two outings at Navan; from a respected stable but seems safe to leave out of calculations.
runners.comment: Twice in rear at Navan, stable respected but seems safe to leave out of calculations

runners.name: Queen Of Newark
runners.spotlight: Weak form last season and never involved over 5f at Dundalk last month; up against it.
runners.comment: Weak form last season and never involved over 5f at Dundalk last month, up against it

runners.name: Six Wings
runners.spotlight: Always at the back when 100-1 chance first time out in a 1m maiden at Naas nine days ago
runners.comment: Always at the back when 100-1 chance first time out in a 1m maiden at Naas nine days ago

runners.name: Schoolyard Days
runners.spotlight: Modest handicap mark (54) after three runs at sprint distances; cheekpieces on now; needs to raise her game.
runners.comment: Modest handicap mark after three runs, needs to raise her game, cheekpieces tried now

races.race_name: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Arizona Raider
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far on Curragh debut; showed the benefit of that run when 2l third of eight over 6f at Navan; his experience should be an asset now; form boosted by runner-up Barrow Boy in winning from the Mulvany-trained The Scallionator at Navan on Saturday.
runners.comment: Third over 6f at Navan, form franked by the second on Saturday, experience an asset here

runners.name: Penny Benjamin
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; sister to 1m winner Glen Princess, half-sister to four winners including Listed scorer Encapsulation; dam 5f AW winner (83),
runners.comment: Eighth foal, sister to 1m winner Glen Princess, half-sister to four winners, dam 5f winner
runners.owner: T Howley Jnr & Eoin Banville & Peter Robin Parkhill

runners.name: Sea Of Rain
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister by Naval Crown to three winners for the stable; dam, a winning daughter of Teofilo; trip should be fine for debut
runners.comment: Eighth foal, half-sister to three winners for the stable, trip should be fine for debut

runners.name: Magma Law
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; half-brother to five winners inc Agrimony (6f/1m inc Australia; RPR 96), Strong And Stable (French 1m1f; 92) and Molly Mischief (1m3f AW; 77); dam unraced sister to Australian 1m5f Group 2 winner Permit
runners.comment: Tenth foal, half-brother to five winners, plenty of stamina in the pedigree

runners.name: Dominic Street
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother to a winner in Italy; dam an unraced half-sister to Group 2 winner Banimpire and to the dam of Melbourne Cup winner Twilight Payment; a rare Flat runner for this jumps stable.
runners.comment: Dam an unraced half-sister to Group 2 winner Banimpire, a rare Flat runner for jumps yard

runners.name: Seaxburh
runners.spotlight: E36,000 yearling; 13th foal; sister to an Italian 2yo winner, half-sister to seven winners; dam won the Rockfel Stakes, out of Oaks/Irish 1,000 Guineas winner Imagine; stable in great form.
runners.comment: Well-bred dam won the Rockfel Stakes and has produced eight winners, stable in great form

runners.name: Glenade
runners.spotlight: E45,000 yearling; second foal; half-brother to 7f 2yo winner Galileo Island; dam 7f/1m AW winner (62), half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Victory Chime; stable in outstanding form.
runners.comment: Second foal; half-brother to 7f 2yo winner Galileo Island; stable in outstanding form

runners.name: Cochise County
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and never in contention when ninth of ten over C&D on debut last Monday.
runners.comment: Slowly away and never in contention when ninth of ten over C&D on debut last Monday
runners.owner: Exors of the Late J C Fleming

races.race_name: Knockcroghery Handicap

runners.name: Thrifty Of Digby
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year, including over the extended 1m2f on this card; her other success was over 1m at Gowran; two moderate runs this term; trip may not be ideal into the bargain.
runners.comment: Won over a longer trip on this card last season, two moderate runs this term

runners.name: Go Out
runners.spotlight: Maiden has done most of his racing on AW, runner-up three times; fair turf form this term, but needs to find extra.
runners.comment: Best form on AW, runner-up three times, fair turf form this term but needs to find extra
runners.owner: Mrs John Murphy & John Pearson & Karl Kirwan

runners.name: Bella Colombia
runners.spotlight: Added a second AW win at Dundalk in March (1m); 0-13 on turf but ran some decent races in defeat in 2024 and competed effectively off this mark when third of 13 over 1m at Limerick last month.
runners.comment: 0-13 on turf but ran well off this mark when third of 13 over 1m at Limerick last month.

runners.name: Royal Impact
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in turf maidens; improved for switch to AW; won on handicap debut over 7f at Dundalk in February off 63; doing his best work at the finish when a 7l fourth of 23 at the Curragh in March (soft to heavy); fair fifth of 21 over 6f at the same venue last time (soft); should appreciate this longer trip and better ground.
runners.comment: 7f winner at Dundalk, should appreciate this longer trip after a good 6f Curragh run

runners.name: Queen Aethelflaed
runners.spotlight: Won on handicap debut off 6lb lower at Thurles last October; backed that up with a decent third at Leopardstown (7f, heavy) later that month and another solid run at Dundalk (10.5f) in December; disappointing when one of the main fancies at Bellewstown last month.
runners.comment: Disappointing when one of the main fancies at Bellewstown, capable of better than that

runners.name: Irish Rumour
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 28 starts; placed in three of her nine races last year, and lost out by only a nose in an 18-runner contest at Fairyhouse in September; fair chance if she can run to that level on her first outing since a satisfactory Dundalk run in November.
runners.comment: Only one win from 28 starts, went very close at Fairyhouse last autumn, top rider booked

runners.name: Blue Anthem
runners.spotlight: Three wins for George Boughey (two on AW); signed off with a 6f AW second at Wolverhampton and bought for 21,000gns last October; unplaced in two Dundalk outings and no better than mid-division in two Curragh runs; tongue-toe added now.
runners.comment: Three wins for George Boughey, hard to be confident based on four Irish outings

runners.name: Free Solo
runners.spotlight: Has been out of form since C&D win last August; signs of a revival with a prominent showing at the Curragh a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Has been out of form since C&D win last August, signs of a revival at the Curragh recently

runners.name: Lohengrin
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Laytown maiden last September; poor winter form at Dundalk; brought down halfway at Bellewstown on return; big price when in rear over a longer trip at Navan on latest; looks vulnerable off this mark.
runners.comment: Only win from 27 starts gained in a 7f maiden at Laytown, his trainer's local track

runners.name: Tara Na Ri
runners.spotlight: Modest form in maidens; finished near the back on handicap debut at the Curragh; cheekpieces added and Colin Keane sticks with him.
runners.comment: Modest form in maidens, finished near the back on handicap debut at the Curragh

runners.name: Zabriskie Point
runners.spotlight: Both wins came as a juvenile for Charlie Hills at 5f/7f on different ground types; has plunged in the ratings and profited from a drop in grade when third at Leopardstown on his penultimate start; unplaced favourite at Gowran last time.
runners.comment: Profited from a drop in grade when third at Leopardstown on his penultimate start
runners.owner: B White & M W White & Ms H McGuinness & D Sheridan

runners.name: Suerte
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; unplaced in six starts on turf; hard to make a case for her, though may improve from last month's seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden, unplaced in six starts on turf, hard to make a case for her

runners.name: Loingseoir
runners.spotlight: Recorded the most recent of his seven wins last autumn; two mid-division runs this season; retains ability; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, recorded the most recent of his seven wins last autumn, retains ability
runners.owner: W Keeling & M C Grassick & Mrs M J Grassick

runners.name: Bucaneer's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Won a Ballinrobe claimer last summer before struggling in handicaps; took advantage of a reduced mark to score over 1m at Cork; down the field in the charity race at Punchestown; fair sixth when 20-1 shot at Leopardstown last Friday.
runners.comment: Won over 1m at Cork last month, fair sixth when 20-1 shot at Leopardstown last Friday

runners.name: Mercurial
runners.spotlight: Only two wins from 40 starts, but placed 16 times; last won at the Curragh (6f, heavy) in April 2024 off 3lb lower; generally below best last year apart from Listowel fourth and Currgah second in the autumn; has a preference for soft/heavy
runners.comment: Only two wins from 40 starts, placed 16 times, has a preference for soft/heavy

runners.name: Famed Again
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when fourth of 16 in valuable sales race at Naas as a juvenile; failed to build on that last season; makes handicap debut from a mark 9lb below his initial rating; might be premature to write off.
runners.comment: Fair form at two, regressive last year, handicap debut, mark is 9lb below initial rating

runners.name: Buttermere Ridge
runners.spotlight: Nothing of any note in her form since early in her career; blinkered now; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, nothing of any note in her form since early in her career, blinkered now

runners.name: Lust
runners.spotlight: Listed winner in 2022 and rated 102 at his peak; form has slumped dramatically in recent seasons; down to 53 now; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, Listed winner in 2022, form has slumped dramatically in recent seasons

races.race_name: Michael Tiger Lyons Memorial Maiden

runners.name: Huff'n'puff
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when keeping on from off the pace in a 1m Curragh maiden last October; confirmed maiden-winning potential with third placing over 1m at Navan on seasonal debut; faded late into fifth over 1m2f at the same venue last time; has much the same chance as Karrygrant on official and RPR figures.
runners.comment: Faded late into fifth over 1m2f at Navan, has much the chance as Karrygrant on figures

runners.name: Karrygrant
runners.spotlight: Promising second on Leopardstown debut last October (7f, yielding to soft); raced keenly and in front over 1f out when run out of things late on at Navan (1m2f, heavy), finishing third; collared late over 1m at Bellewstown last time; stays well despite a pedigree more geared to shorter distances.
runners.comment: Placed on all three starts, stays well despite a pedigree more geared to shorter distances

runners.name: Gaelic Drills
runners.spotlight: Two runs of roughly the same standard at Leopardstown and Cork; needs to find more.
runners.comment: Two runs of roughly the same standard at Leopardstown and Cork, needs to find more

runners.name: Noble Honour
runners.spotlight: Mixed signals from the form of her Gowran second last September; winner Ice Dancer has not trained on, third-placed Mixed Feelings has won a maiden but was well beaten when raised in class; one for the shortlist despite reservations.
runners.comment: Mixed signals from the form of her Gowran second last September, one for the shortlist

runners.name: Changing Lanes
runners.spotlight: E125,000 yearling; improvement on the cards after a respectable debut over 1m4f at Dundalk, third of seven after running prominently throughout; stable in terrific form.
runners.comment: Improvement on the cards after a respectable debut at Dundalk, stable in terrific form

runners.name: Ryans Hope
runners.spotlight: Third on only start for Iain Jardine in a Newcastle maiden in February 2024; no impact on recent Irish debut; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, placed in a small-field event in Britain, no impact on recent Irish debut

runners.name: Solar Jet
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-brother to winners Rainbow Jet (10.4f; RPR 78) and Ice Jet (2m3f hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to useful 5f/6f winner Came From The Dark.
runners.comment: Eighth foal, half-brother to two winners, newcomer faces several with decent form

runners.name: Galileo Springs
runners.spotlight: Placed in three of her four bumper starts, including a fine effort behind the useful Carrigmoornaspruce; winner over hurdles on yielding ground at Killarney last autumn; younger rivals preferred.
runners.comment: Placed in three of his four bumper starts, winner over hurdles, younger rivals preferred

runners.name: Elzaam Express
runners.spotlight: Tailed off last of five over C&D last week on debut; can be discounted.
runners.comment: Tailed off last of five over C&D last week on debut, can be discounted

runners.name: Believe Again
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a Listowel bumper last September on his only start; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a Listowel bumper last September on his only start, safe to rule out

runners.name: It's Good To Talk
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to five winners, notably smart Flat performer Raise You and Champion Bumper winner A Dream To Share; has run in two bumpers; second at Naas on debut, failed to shine at the Punchestown festival.
runners.comment: Second in a Naas bumper on debut, failed to shine at the Punchestown festival

runners.name: Zelani
runners.spotlight: Fair fifth over 1m at Navan on debut and improvement on the cards over this longer trip; has almost 5l to find with third-placed Huff'n'puff.
runners.comment: Fair fifth over 1m at Navan on debut, improvement on the cards over this longer trip
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Matada
runners.spotlight: Has failed to make the first ten in maidens at Navan and Gowran; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, has failed to make the first ten in maidens at Navan and Gowran

runners.name: Avec Grand Plaisir
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in maidens at the Curragh and Leopardstown five months apart; will qualify for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in maidens at the Curragh and Leopardstown five months apart

runners.name: Garryowen Green
runners.spotlight: Towards the back at Dundalk last December; no better at Limerick last month; tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Towards the back at Dundalk last December, no better at Limerick last month, tongue-tie

runners.name: African Affair
runners.spotlight: No apparent chance judged on his first two starts; well held by Huff'n'puff on Naas run.
runners.comment: No apparent chance judged on his first two starts, well held by Huff'nPuff on Naas run

runners.name: Grennanstown Lady
runners.spotlight: Showed little over hurdles last year; well beaten in two Flat starts this term; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, showed little over hurdles last year, well beaten in two Flat starts

races.race_name: Connolly's RED MILLS Handicap

runners.name: Queen Lioness
runners.spotlight: Ran once for Johnny Murtagh in 2023; overcame an absence of more than 1,000 days to win at Limerick last month; runner-up Treasure Rose took the same placing at Killarney last week; hard to assess, but could be competitive here with 7lb claimed.
runners.comment: Ran once in 2023, overcame prolonged absence to win at Limerick, could be competitive here

runners.name: Syzygy
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive from a low base last autumn, winning all three handicaps on heavy ground, at Gowran and twice at the Curragh; continued improvement on return when fine third of 25 from 7lb out of handicap in Irish Lincoln in first-time cheekpieces; coped with good-to-yielding ground when third at Leopardstown last time; any ease would help.
runners.comment: Highly progressive last autumn, has maintained her profile in two outings this season

runners.name: Follow Me
runners.spotlight: No success since back-to-back wins at the Curragh in 2024; no impact in three runs last season; mid-field on reappearance at Limerick; stable's form is a positive.
runners.comment: Back-to-back wins in 2024, has tended to struggle since then, stable's form is a positive

runners.name: Kilcrea Rock
runners.spotlight: Promise in two runs at two, notably when second to the subsequent 2025 Irish Derby runner-up Serious Contender; uneven form last year; just denied at Leopardstown on handicap/seasonal debut; up 4lb but still a leading contender for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Just denied at Leopardstown on handicap/seasonal debut, leading contender for in-form yard

runners.name: Gotta Catch'em All
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 23 runs; placed seven times up to 1m4f; two respectable runs this season, plenty of horses behind him on both occasions, seventh of 17 at Leopardstown and sixth of 23 at Navan; hard to be confident but may reward the persistence of connections at some point.
runners.comment: Maiden after 23 runs; placed seven times, two respectable runs this season, place chance
runners.owner: Mrs S Bury & Ralph Kelly & Odhran O'Neill & Thomas Cox

runners.name: Electric Beauty
runners.spotlight: Has been busy this year and was fit from AW when winning over 1m at the Curragh in March; touched off on her return there before disappointing when favourite at Gowran; has made the running on her two most recent starts; may find a few too good again.
runners.comment: Began the season with a Curragh win, has been kept busy, may find a few too good

runners.name: Little Miss P
runners.spotlight: Fair form in maidens last year; disappointing on handicap/seasonal debut at Leopardstown; others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Disappointing on handicap/seasonal debut at Leopardstown, others have stronger claims

runners.name: Rising Sky
runners.spotlight: Dundalk winner in March, her first success at the 15th attempt; faced a tough task from out of the handicap at Musselburgh last month; her best form has been for this rider.
runners.comment: Dundalk winner in March, faced a tough task from out of the handicap at Musselburgh

runners.name: Lahore Da King
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back AW winner in December over 7f; improved on previous turf form when landing a Bellewstown handicap (1m, soft) last month; well beaten at the Curragh more recently.
runners.comment: Useful form at Dundalk, well beaten at the Curragh since a first turf win at Bellewstown

runners.name: Sir Jeremy
runners.spotlight: Sole win for Ado McGuinness came in a 7f Down Royal nursery in September 2024; uneven form last year; needs to improve from stable debut at the Curragh in March.
runners.comment: Nursery winner, uneven form last year; needs to improve from stable debut at the Curragh

runners.name: Oust
runners.spotlight: Appeared not to stay 1m5f at Ballinrobe last time; this may be closer to his best trip and he is relatively unexposed.
runners.comment: Appeared not to stay 1m5f at Ballinrobe last time, this may be closer to his best trip

runners.name: Robot Rock
runners.spotlight: Placed on AW in France; lost ground at the start at this venue last week on Irish debut; 40-1 then, best watched for the moment.
runners.comment: Placed on AW in France, lost ground at the start at this venue on Irish debut, 40-1 then

races.race_name: Abbey Hotel Handicap

runners.name: Wipeawayyourtears
runners.spotlight: Good start in handicap company when keeping on into second on her return at Sligo (10.5f, yielding to soft); mark left alone.
runners.comment: Good start in handicap company when keeping on into second at Sligo (10.5f);

runners.name: Onyeisi
runners.spotlight: Busy on AW during the winter for Charlie Johnston, winning over 1m2f and second over this trip; picked up for 14,000gns in February.
runners.comment: Busy on AW during the winter for Charlie Johnston, winner over 1m2f, second over this trip

runners.name: Survivor's Code
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f at Gowran last month, held since; kept on over 9.5f at Ballinrobe when tried in blinkers; might stay but others preferred.
runners.comment: Kept on over 9.5f at Ballinrobe when tried in blinkers; might stay but others preferred

runners.name: Tenacious
runners.spotlight: Missed a good chance to win one when hung left late on at Gowran, finishing third over 1m; 3-1 fav when turned out three days later at Ballinrobe, ran a stinker; cheekpieces tried then left off; some stamina on dam's side of pedigree so might stay, but prefer to look elsewhere, as does Foley.
runners.comment: Hung left at Gowran when finishing third over 1m; bombed out when fav at Ballinrobe

runners.name: Sea Of Dubka
runners.spotlight: Unfancied both handicap starts but has run well; just behind Musashi when third at Dundalk (10.5f) and form of her front-running 2l fourth over this trip at Cork has worked out well; down in grade too.
runners.comment: Form of her front-running 2l fourth over this trip at Cork has worked out well
runners.owner: Anthony Head & Stephen E McCarthy & Mrs Paul Shanahan

runners.name: Musashi
runners.spotlight: Was just in front of Sea Of Dubka when second on seasonal/handicap debut on AW (10.5f); 7-2 fav, outpaced turning for home before keeping on well on inner (not much room) when chasing home Femme Beauty at Killarney (1m3f) last week; extra furlong should be fine.
runners.comment: Kept on well on inner (not much room) when chasing home Femme Beauty at Killarney (1m3f)

runners.name: Femme Beauty
runners.spotlight: Got a good ride from the front on speed-favouring track when holding on to beat Musashi at Killarney; 3lb worse off and wouldn't fancy her to confirm the form over this extra furlong.
runners.comment: Got a good ride from the front on speed-favouring track when  beating Musashi at Killarney

runners.name: Captain Con
runners.spotlight: Quiet runs in maidens and now steps up in trip for handicap debut; by a miler and fair bit of speed on dam's side.
runners.comment: Quiet runs in maidens and now steps up in trip for handicap debut; 1m4f not sure to suit

runners.name: Retro Gal
runners.spotlight: Never competitive in three maiden outing over 1m; half-sister to 1m2f AW winner and is out of a Nathaniel mare, so good chance this trip will suit; handicap debut and looks yard second-string, but not one to dismiss by any means.
runners.comment: Never competitive in three maiden outing over 1m; half-sister to 1m2f AW winner

runners.name: Adel
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably in Madrid Handicap over 7f on return, weakened over 1m next time but perhaps that came too soon; up in trip and down in grade now, and gets back onto better ground.
runners.comment: Ran respectably in Madrid Handicap over 7f on return, weakened over 1m next time

runners.name: Arizona Star
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way all three starts, including when 400-1 third over 1m2f at the Curragh; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way all three starts, including when 400-1 third at the Curragh; h'cap bow

runners.name: Lilo Pelekai
runners.spotlight: Modest handicap form and when fifth in 9.5f claimer at Ballinrobe; doubt that 1m4f will be the answer; 2lb wrong.
runners.comment: Modest handicap form and when fifth in 9.5f claimer at Ballinrobe; doubt 1m4f will suit

runners.name: Connecteo
runners.spotlight: Quiet runs in 7f/1m maidens but related to several winners over this trip and not hard to envisage a much-improved effort on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Quiet runs in 7f/1m maidens, but related to several winners over this trip; handicap debut

runners.name: Admiral Will Brown
runners.spotlight: Beaten around 12l on first two starts, in decent maidens won by Ballydoyle juveniles; has been gelded and steps up in trip for handicap debut; half-brother to 1m4f winner and dam won over 1m6f; trainer had third in Killarney contest involving two of the principals here, so has a line to the form.
runners.comment: Gelded and steps up in trip for handicap debut; half-brother to 1m4f winner

races.race_name: Racing Again Here On June 8th Handicap

runners.name: Radar Ahead
runners.spotlight: Consistent 5yo has won over hurdles; still a maiden on the Flat, but comes here in good form; third in big field at Navan and over C&D a week ago; up in grade here.
runners.comment: Consistent 5yo still a maiden on the Flat, but comes here in good form, third here latest

runners.name: Obscenity
runners.spotlight: Solid runs in first three handicaps, all on easy ground; 7-4 fav, long way behind runner-up Superficial when third over 1m3f at Bellewstown; left that behind when bolting up in 23-runner contest at Navan (1m2f); bred to stay this far, but still a bit of a question mark over his stamina.
runners.comment: Won readily over 1m2f at Navan, beating 22 rivals; long way off Superficial time before

runners.name: Out On Friday
runners.spotlight: Front-runner loves it here; won this last year and caught late when second over C&D in August; lack of a prep run is the concern, because his record fresh is not good.
runners.comment: Front-runner loves it here and won this last year; lack of a prep run is the concern

runners.name: Cooley's Mist
runners.spotlight: Fourth of 21 over 1m1f on handicap debut at Leopardstown was his best run for Natalia Lupini; disappointed on heavy ground next time and has been off eight months; gelded.
runners.comment: Fourth of 21 over 1m1f at Leopardstown was his best run for Natalia Lupini; off 8 months

runners.name: Amplitude
runners.spotlight: Was hit hard by the handicapper for easily winning a restricted 9.5f maiden at Gowran on heavy in September; good run when fourth over 1m2f at Leopardstown last month and seemed to stay well enough when third over 1m5f at Ballinrobe, getting going a bit late; longer straight here will suit and any rain a plus.
runners.comment: Seemed to get the trip well when third over 1m5f at Ballinrobe; any rain a plus

runners.name: Superficial
runners.spotlight: Some good efforts in soft-ground handicaps, including seasonal-debut second at Bellewstown (1m3f) when clear of third Obscenity; not at his best at Ballinrobe last time when behind a couple of these.
runners.comment: Some good efforts in handicaps, clear of third Obscenity when second at Bellewstown

runners.name: God Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles and on the Flat at Limerick last year; well exposed in this discipline; money for him over C&D last week when showed a bit more than of late, beaten around 6l; up in grade here.
runners.comment: Money for him over C&D last week when beaten around 6l; up in grade and more needed

runners.name: La Dame Blanche
runners.spotlight: Had good form two years ago, notably landing the Cork Derby; Tramore second was her only worthwhile effort last year; has come back down the ratings and better for recent AW return.
runners.comment: Had good form two years ago and has come back down the ratings; better for AW comeback

runners.name: Lily Parr
runners.spotlight: Maiden not had much racing and couple of good runs last autumn, fifth in useful 1m3f handicap at Killarney and fourth in big-field 1m4f maiden at the Curragh; off six months.
runners.comment: Maiden not had much racing and couple of good runs last year 1m3f/1m4f; off six months

runners.name: Mr Rango
runners.spotlight: Long time since latest of three wins but he's off a good mark nowadays; place behind Amplitude over 1m5f at Ballinrobe last time; rain would help his cause.
runners.comment: Long time since latest of three wins; place behind Amplitude over 1m5f at Ballinrobe

runners.name: Pavilion End
runners.spotlight: Has only had one Flat run on turf; fair performer over this trip on AW at Dundalk, maiden winner and good third off this mark in handicap in October; wouldn't want it to turn soft.
runners.comment: Maiden winner over this trip on AW and good third in handicap in October; quiet since

runners.name: Silent Guardian
runners.spotlight: Had some good efforts on the Flat for Emmet Mullins, showing early promise in 3yo maidens and  just behind Pavilion End when fourth in 1m4f AW handicap in October; won 2m3f handicap hurdle at Clonmel in March.
runners.comment: Hurdles winner in March and was just behind Pavilion End in 1m4f AW handicap in October

races.race_name: Roscommon Handicap

runners.name: Miss Australie
runners.spotlight: Yet to place in 14 starts but one of her better runs was over C&D last year and bit of encouragement last time at Ballinrobe, never a factor after being baulked from stalls but some late headway over 9.5f.
runners.comment: Bit of encouragement last time at Ballinrobe when some late headway over 9.5f

runners.name: Cuckaloo Hill
runners.spotlight: Twice placed for Paul Nolan in good-ground handicaps last June; best run so far for current yard when fourth over 9.5f at Ballinrobe, with four of these behind; unproven over this far, though.
runners.comment: Best run for current yard when fourth over 9.5f at Ballinrobe, with four of these behind

runners.name: Deluca Chop
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins in France back in the day; sole Irish win came on AW over three years ago; found his form again in recent months at Dundalk, placed three times over this trip and when beaten a length over 2m last time; rated lower on turf.
runners.comment: Has found his form again in recent months at Dundalk and he's rated lower on turf

runners.name: Idomything
runners.spotlight: Fourth over C&D on stable debut before winning on soft ground at Bellewstown in October; well beaten since, but probably needed the run when long way behind Prom Queen at Gowran.
runners.comment: Winner over 1m4f on soft ground at Bellewstown in October; well beaten since

runners.name: Kartayaz
runners.spotlight: Five wins, four of them on AW including 50-1 success under this rider in December; shaped like the run was needed at Ballinrobe last time; best form is over 10.5f, this trip stretches him
runners.comment: Shaped like the run was needed at Ballinrobe last time; best form is over 10.5f,

runners.name: Sayidah New Bay
runners.spotlight: Nice pedigree and beaten less than a length on handicap debut at Killarney in October (1m3f, soft); has kept on over shorter trips last twice and now steps back up in trip.
runners.comment: Maiden has kept on over shorter trips last twice and now steps back up in trip.

runners.name: Prom Queen
runners.spotlight: Front-runner off the mark over 1m6f at Gowran, off basement Irish Flat rating of 40; fifth won at Leopardstown on Friday; went left up the straight last time and trainer feels she'll be better going that way, so might struggle on the bends here.
runners.comment: Front-runner off the mark over 1m6f at Gowran, off basement Irish Flat rating of 40

runners.name: Piatra Neamt
runners.spotlight: Ran okay over 1m2f on handicap debut at Navan last October but twice well held over this trip after; always behind at Ballinrobe last time; cheekpieces swapped for visor.
runners.comment: Ran okay over 1m2f on handicap debut at Navan last October; well held since; visor

runners.name: Tassarolo
runners.spotlight: First reserve; three wins last year, two of them over hurdles; third in 1m4f Curragh handicap in March;  another good effort when fifth of 19 at Leopardstown on Friday.
runners.comment: Another good effort when fifth of 19 at Leopardstown on Friday; first reserve

runners.name: Al What
runners.spotlight: Has been beaten 10 lengths+ in all five handicap starts; had a recent spin over hurdles.
runners.comment: Has been beaten 10 lengths+ in all five handicap starts; had a recent spin over hurdles

runners.name: Mapa
runners.spotlight: Maiden was good fifth over 1m at the Curragh in October; then stepped back up in trip to 10.5f on AW, best run when second in November; no impact in one previous attempt over this far; headgear left off.
runners.comment: Second over 10.5f on AW in November; no impact on ine previous attempt over this far

runners.name: Grey Fable
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back wins at Chester in 2024, but has never won on home soil; moderate form last year and same story in two recent outings; 1m7f too far latest.
runners.comment: Back-to-back wins at Chester in 2024; moderate form last year and same story recently

runners.name: Whosethebestboy
runners.spotlight: Moderate form for Charles Byrnes, dead in the market when tailed off on handicap debut in August; owner likes to shuffle the pack and has sent 6yo to another sharp operator; tongue-tie and champion jockey.
runners.comment: Moderate form for Charles Byrnes; champion jockey aboard for stable debut; tongue-tie

runners.name: If Dora Could
runners.spotlight: Fair effort on just third handicap start when fifth over 10.5f on AW; tongue-tie tried then stays on; was keen the last day, so trip could be an issue, but is a half-sister to 1m4f winner.
runners.comment: Fair effort on just third handicap start when fifth over 10.5f on AW; this trip a concern

runners.name: Edge Of Darkness
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over this trip back in the day in Britain; Irish form is moderate in both codes, second in claimer here in 2024; a tad better when beaten 6l (led) at Navan latest and Keane rides.
runners.comment: Irish form is moderate in both codes, a tad better when beaten 6l (led) at Navan latest

runners.name: Burlington House
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in all eight Flat starts in Britain; progressed in handicap hurdles late last year, last seen when second at Limerick over Christmas.
runners.comment: Unplaced all eight Flat starts in Britain; progressed in handicap hurdles late last year

runners.name: Jack Major
runners.spotlight: Got handicapped in sprint maidens; tailed off on handicap debut over 7.5f in August; half-brother to pair of 1m2f winners, but has to be a doubtful stayer over this far; back with original trainer.
runners.comment: Poor form; half-brother 1m2f winners, but has to be a doubtful stayer over this far

runners.name: Church Mountain
runners.spotlight: Winner of nine races; form has been uneven since 1m4f Dundalk success in January; down the field at Leopardstown on Friday, but never one to rule out in this grade.
runners.comment: Winner of nine races; down the field at Leopardstown on Friday; second reserve

races.race_name: Dine In The Wolverhampton Horizons Restaurant Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Magna
runners.spotlight: Just keeps on progressing at 6f and 5f and landed a three-timer here (6f) 13 days ago; her small winning margins means she doesn't get raised too much and there is no reason why she should not be in the firing line again.
runners.comment: Just keeps on progressing and brought up a three-timer 13 days ago, over 6f here

runners.name: Star Marian
runners.spotlight: Showed first bit of form when successful in a maiden over C&D in March; didn't appear to get home when fifth of six upped to 6f here since; steps back in trip but needs to show that win was not just a one-off.
runners.comment: Won maiden over C&D in March; didn't appear to get home when fifth here (6f) last month.

runners.name: Midnight Call
runners.spotlight: Promise on turf last summer and has made good progress on AW since November, winning five times here (three over today's distance); came up short over C&D last month and the handicapper may now have her measure.
runners.comment: Has made good progress on AW since November, winning five here; held on latest start

runners.name: Smooth Silesie
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark at Yarmouth (5f, good to soft) last October but hasn't got going in four runs this year, beating only one rival; needs to show more.
runners.comment: Won off this mark at Yarmouth (5f) in October but hasn't got going in four runs this year.

runners.name: Rotokura Belle
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent but returned to her best after three below-par efforts when successful at Catterick (5f, good) last month; suffered interference when only sixth there since but is not one to place maximum faith in.
runners.comment: Returned to her best when winning at Catterick (5f) last month; hampered there since.

runners.name: Powernap
runners.spotlight: Has shown little on turf so far; makes AW debut and needs to show improvement.
runners.comment: Has shown little on turf so far; makes AW debut and needs to show improvement.

runners.name: Fantasy Obsessor
runners.spotlight: Won two AW novice events last summer over 6f-7f; hasn't gone on in handicaps since; first-time cheekpieces go on now she steps down in class back at 5f off a plummeting mark; may well show more.
runners.comment: Landed a couple of AW novice events last summer over 6f-7f but poor since.

runners.name: Little Jaybee
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in a maiden over C&D last June on her second start; well held in handicaps on her last three starts; eased in grade here but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Won maiden over C&D last June; well held in handicaps on her last three starts.
runners.owner: Mrs A L Lofts & Mrs Nicky Scott Knight

races.race_name: Cavani Menswear Style Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Undercover Affair
runners.spotlight: Has ran respectably in finishing fourth in his two runs to date; makes AW debut and should not be far away.
runners.comment: Has ran respectably in finishing fourth in his two runs to date; makes AW debut.

runners.name: Agamemnon
runners.spotlight: Mehmas colt who lost his race at the start when slowly away on his debut at Newmarket (5f, good to firm; 4-1) 15 days ago; makes AW debut and may be a different proposition if he gets away on terms.
runners.comment: Slowly away on his debut at Newmarket 15 days ago; may be different proposition here

runners.name: Najmet Minzaal
runners.spotlight: 50,000euros yearling; tenth foal, by Minzaal; half-sister to seven winners including Fox Power (7f-8.6f AW including 2yo/Listed; RPR 106), Needlepoint (5.5f 2yo/6f AW; 88), Dark Crescent (7f 2yo; 82) and Always Fearless (7f-8.6f including 2yo/AW; 80); dam 6f and 1m (Listed) 2yo winner (94); stable going well; interesting contender who needs a check in the betting.
runners.comment: Well connected and needs a check in the betting with  stable going well.

runners.name: American Psycho
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to four juvenile winners; makes a quick reappearance after an encouraging debut second at Salisbury (5f, good) last Thursday; should go well on his AW debut.
runners.comment: Makes a quick reappearance after an encouraging debut second at Salisbury last Thursday.

runners.name: Katie's Cavalier
runners.spotlight: 17,000euros yearling; £15,000 breeze-up 2yo; fourth foal; by Kodi Bear; half-brother to 7f-1m2f AW winner Flaine (including 2yo; RPR 73); dam unplaced 9.5f-1m4f, half-sister to Listed-placed French winners Pretty Panther (6.5f-1m) and Plaisanciere (6.5f/7f); worth a market check but might be better for the run and a longer trip.
runners.comment: Worth market check but might be better for the run and a longer trip

runners.name: Bill The Bull
runners.spotlight: 17,000gns yearling; April foal; showed plenty of speed before finishing fourth of 15 (beaten 4l) in the Brocklesby at Doncaster (5f, good to soft; 8-1) in March; holds good claims switched to the AW.
runners.comment: Showed plenty of speed before finishing fourth in the Brocklesby at Doncaster in March.

runners.name: Drum Major
runners.spotlight: Beaten 15l and 11l on his two starts so far and needs to take a massive step forward; first-time cheekpieces are now applied.
runners.comment: Beaten 15l and 11l on his two starts so far and needs to take a massive step forward.

races.race_name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mountain Cat
runners.spotlight: Showed inexperience leaving the stalls but ultimately ran out a comfortable winner on his debut at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) 16 days ago; makes AW debut and should prove hard to beat.
runners.comment: Ran out a comfortable winner on his debut at Doncaster 16 days ago; hard to beat.

runners.name: Rogue Defence
runners.spotlight: Made a perfect start to his career when winning at Lingfield AW (1m) last month; looks sure to progress and should be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: Perfect start to his career when winning at Lingfield AW (1m) last month; in the shake-up

runners.name: Zabeel Small
runners.spotlight: 7,000euros yearling; fifth foal, by Lucky Vega; half-brother to 7f-1m2f winner Euro Nightmare (including 2yo/Listed; RPR 100); dam unraced half-sister to 6f/7f winner Half A Billion, out of 9.6f winning half-sister to 1m2f Group 2 winner Batshoof; stable among the winners; check the betting.
runners.comment: Stable among the winner; worth a check in the betting on this debut.

runners.name: Leopards Rock
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; by Territories; dam 5f winner (including 2yo/AW; RPR 73), half-sister to useful 5f-7f winner Secretinthepark and prolific winners Dolphin Rock (7f-1m2f) and Somewhere Secret (5f/6f);   might be better for this debut but the market can guide.
runners.comment: Quite well connected but might be better for the run; market can guide

runners.name: Roi De Coeur
runners.spotlight: Stable is going well but this one looks held by Rogue Defence on their running at Lingfield (1m; 6-1) last month on his debut.
runners.comment: Looks held by Rogue Defence on their running at Lingfield (1m) last month.

runners.name: Arc At Her
runners.spotlight: Last of nine on debut at Goodwood (1m, good) 16 days ago; has a lot to prove on this AW debut.
runners.comment: Last of nine on debut at Goodwood 16 days ago; lots to find.
runners.owner: Gee Gee's Racing & Hold My Beer

runners.name: Rua Mor
runners.spotlight: Showed little in a maiden and four spins over hurdles in Ireland; sold to join current yard for 12,000euros in February; makes stable debut and unlikely to be seen to best effect until he runs in handicaps.
runners.comment: Makes stable debut and unlikely to be seen to best effect until he runs in handicaps.

races.race_name: Sky Sports Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: City Queen
runners.spotlight: Steadily improving and got off the mark on her handicap debut at Southwell (1m, AW) last month; should continue on an upward curve and shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Steadily improving and got off the mark on her handicap debut at Southwell last month.

runners.name: Symbol Of Majesty
runners.spotlight: Remains unbeaten after winning on her turf debut at Haydock (1m, good to firm) last month; highly unexposed on this handicap debut and commands respect.
runners.comment: Remains unbeaten after winning on her turf debut at Haydock last month.

runners.name: Estissa
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over C&D in March, but was surprisingly turned over at short odds when second of four back over C&D last month; makes handicap debut and may be worth another chance.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in March but beaten at short odds back here last month.

runners.name: Morven
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on her second start, at Haydock (1m, good to soft) last September; likely found the step up in grade too hot when last of five in a Listed contest at Pontefract (1m, soft) the following month; makes AW/handicap debut and worth a market check.
runners.comment: Won on her second start, at Haydock last September; last in better company subsequently.

runners.name: Silken Bay
runners.spotlight: 50-1 winner on her second start, at Southwell (1m, AW) last month in a first-time hood (retained since); showed that was no fluke when third to Symbol Of Majesty on her turf debut at Haydock (1m, good to firm) subsequently and gets 7lb pull with that rival; not out of this on handicap debut.
runners.comment: 50-1 winner on her second start, at Southwell last month; third at Haydock since.

runners.name: Lyra Lea
runners.spotlight: Showed her best form last year when winning a Lingfield maiden (7f, AW) in August, before being turned over in three handicaps; steps up in trip and needs to hit the boards running here on first run this year.
runners.comment: Won a Lingfield maiden last August before being turned over in three handicaps.

runners.name: Golden Mabel
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and well beaten in three fillies' novices this year; makes no appeal on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Huge prices and well beaten in three fillies' novices this year.

races.race_name: Country & Western Raceday 28th June Handicap

runners.name: Karakula
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D off this mark last September; encouraging second over C&D last month on reappearance and the booking of Oisin Murphy may be significant; solid claims.
runners.comment: Second over C&D last month and the booking of Oisin Murphy may be significant.

runners.name: Silkies Sib
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D in December (off 3lb lower) and at Chelmsford in February; has run respectably since, including when beaten favourite at Bath last time; remains on a competitive mark and won't be far away.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D in December (off 3lb lower) and at Chelmsford in February.

runners.name: Stipulation
runners.spotlight: Completed a brace when scoring over C&D last September off 4lb lower; has run consistently since and looks capable of winning another race such as this.
runners.comment: Completed a brace when scoring over C&D last September off 4lb lower.
runners.owner: Horn And Trumpet Lads & S & A Mares

runners.name: Dingwall
runners.spotlight: Scored back-to-back wins here (one over today's distance) last November; has finished runner-up on his last four starts and is capable of winning off this mark.
runners.comment: Has finished runner-up on his last four starts and is capable of winning off this mark.

runners.name: The Ubermensch
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but ran his best race last time when second of eight at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) two weeks ago; sports a first-time tongue-tie and should not be far away.
runners.comment: 0-6 but ran his best race last time when second at Windsor two weeks ago.

runners.name: Age Of Baroque
runners.spotlight: 7f winner at Southwell for Marco Botti last summer off 3lb higher; patchy since for his current yard; has dropped to a career-low mark but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 7f winner at Southwell for Marco Botti last summer; patchy since for his current yard.

runners.name: Between Me And U
runners.spotlight: Last win was in September 2024 but ran well on several occasions when making the frame since; last seen when a beaten favourite at Nottingham last August and may be better for the run.
runners.comment: Last win was in September 2024 but ran well on several occasions since.

runners.name: Bizou
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form to get off the mark at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) 11 days ago; 5lb higher here and should not be far away.
runners.comment: Showed improved form to get off the mark at Windsor 11 days ago.

races.race_name: Cavani Tailored Champion Handicap

runners.name: Court Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on her seasonal/handicap debut at Kempton (7f, AW) two weeks ago; open to further progress and Oisin Murphy rides for the first time; strong claims.
runners.comment: Got off the mark on her seasonal/handicap debut at Kempton two weeks ago.

runners.name: Seeing Stars
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on her first three starts and did not need to improve to open her account at Kempton (7f, AW) in January; has had a break since and makes handicap debut; should run well.
runners.comment: Did not need to improve to open her account at Kempton in January.

runners.name: Jowalla
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden; showed encouragement when placed on her first three starts last summer but has finished last since in a couple of handicaps; first-time hood replaces cheekpieces and needs to return to form.
runners.comment: Placed on her first three starts last summer but has finished last twice since.

runners.name: Espona Bay
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame over 6f on his last four starts, running up to his best when third of eight at Newcastle in March; steps back up in trip and again shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Has made the frame on his last four starts, running well when third at Newcastle in March.

runners.name: Horwich
runners.spotlight: Won twice over C&D for Clive Cox; sold for 20,000gns to join current yard in February and has run moderately in handicaps since; easy to oppose.
runners.comment: Won twice over C&D for Clive Cox; has run moderately in handicaps for new yard.

runners.name: Dontwaste A Moment
runners.spotlight: Just modest form in three runs over 6f; sports first-time tongue-tie up in trip on his AW/handicap debut; best watched unless the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: Just modest form in three runs over 6f; AW/handicap debut.

runners.name: Okiru
runners.spotlight: 0-8; sent handicapping on his last four starts; ran well in the first two of those before a lesser show in second-time cheekpieces at Lingfield; unseated rider when jumping path on turf debut at Yarmouth last time; has questions to answer.
runners.comment: 0-8; unseated rider when jumping path on turf debut at Yarmouth last time.

runners.name: Perfect Location
runners.spotlight: Won on her second start, at Southwell (6f, AW) last July; shaped as if needing the run on her return when sixth of eight at Kempton (6f, AW) last month before finishing runner-up back there in first-time tongue-tie (retained); steps up in trip and should not be far away.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Kempton last time; steps up in trip and should not be far away.
runners.owner: Mildmay Racing Pl & Only Fillies Rc

runners.name: The Anthony Gover
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on all three starts; sports first-time blinkers on handicap debut; steps down in trip and needs to show significant improvement.
runners.comment: Well beaten on all three starts; needs to show significant improvement.

runners.name: Sprinting Speed
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three runs for Richard Hannon last summer and has showed little for current yard in two runs last month.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all five starts; hard to fancy.

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Kelly Burn
runners.spotlight: 0-5 and her followers got their fingers burnt when she was a beaten favourite, albeit running well on the figures, over C&D last month; however, there should be more to come from her.
runners.comment: Ran well when favourite over C&D last month; there should be more to come from her.

runners.name: Romantic Spirit
runners.spotlight: Won four times last year, three coming at this track over 9.5f; may come on for her recent run at Lingfield last month and may not be far away.
runners.comment: May come on for her recent run at Lingfield last month and may not be far away.

runners.name: Desert Belle
runners.spotlight: Beaten at 10-11 on her debut, she made amends when stepped up in trip to score at Southwell (1m4f) last month; makes handicap debut and will be hard to beat.
runners.comment: Stepped up in trip to score at Southwell (1m4f) last month; makes handicap debut.

runners.name: You Got To My Soul
runners.spotlight: Third at Southwell AW on her first two starts but disappointing on her turf debut at Nottingham; steps up in trip and sports first-time cheekpieces on this return to AW on handicap debut; unexposed and may have more to offer.
runners.comment: Third at Southwell AW on her first two starts but disappointing on turf at Nottingham.

runners.name: Free Speech
runners.spotlight: Three wins for Ed Dunlop; sold for 5,000gns last October; denied a run on several occasions on her stable debut at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) 17 days ago; will be suited by the step back up in trip and not out of this.
runners.comment: Denied a run on several occasions on her stable debut at Newcastle 17 days ago.

runners.name: Pyleates
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on all of her three starts last year; needs to hit the boards running on her handicap debut here.
runners.comment: Made the frame on all of her three starts last year; handicap debut.
